facts.claim: IVF children constituted approximately 3.6% of all children born in Sweden in 2013, with approximately 4,000 babies born from IVF treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, page 7)
facts.body: The report states approximately 4,000 babies born and approximately 3.6% of all births in Sweden. These are approximate figures from the registry summary text.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against summary text on page 7 of qivf-2015-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The mean age of women undergoing IVF treatment in Sweden in 2013 was 33.5 years, with three out of four women younger than 38 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 1 text, page 8)
facts.body: Age distribution: <25 years 2.8%, 25-29 years 15.0%, 30-34 years 32.9%, 35-37 years 22.3%, 38-39 years 15.4%, 40-41 years 8.1%, >41 years 3.5%. 17% were younger than 30 and 11% were older than 40.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 1 pie chart data labels and explanation text in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 8.

facts.claim: The overall multiple birth rate for IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2013 was 4.7% (176 multiple deliveries out of 3,783 total deliveries), among the lowest in the world (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Tables 2a-2b, page 19)
facts.body: Fresh IVF: 5.2% (131/2512). Freeze cycles: 3.5% (45/1271). Combined: 4.65% rounded to 4.7%. The report notes the multiple birth rate in Sweden is very low from an international perspective and has been at a steady and stable level for several years.

facts.claim: In the 2015 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey across 15 of 16 Swedish IVF clinics (N=2,905), satisfaction rates were: medical care 92%, accessibility 82%, information 85%, approach/treatment 92%, and participation in decisions 70% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 17, page 25)
facts.body: All five satisfaction domains improved compared to the 2013 survey (medical care 90%, accessibility 80%, information 81%, approach 90%, participation 67%). IVF-KUPP is a validated instrument specifically adapted for IVF patients. Scores combine responses from both women and men. Participation/shared decision-making scored lowest and showed the most variation across clinics (54%-83%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 17 in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 25. Bar chart with data labels.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2013 was 77.0%, with double embryo transfer (DET) at 22.9% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 5a, page 12)
facts.body: Sweden has the highest SET rates in the world. Since 2003, single embryo transfer has been the standard per National Board of Health and Welfare regulations. Two-embryo transfer is permitted only when the risk of twins is judged to be small.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 5a in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 12. Pie chart with data labels.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for freeze cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2013 was 92.2%, with double embryo transfer (DET) at 7.7% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 5b, page 12)
facts.body: SET rate for frozen cycles was substantially higher than for fresh cycles (92.2% vs 77.0%), reflecting the lower-risk profile of frozen embryo transfer and the principle that SET combined with subsequent FET can achieve similar pregnancy rates to DET with lower complication risk.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 5b in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 12. Pie chart with data labels.

facts.claim: The overall SET rate across all IVF treatments in Sweden in 2013 was approximately 75%, continuing a steady increase from about 10% in 2000 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 14, page 21)
facts.body: Figure 14 trend chart shows the dramatic rise in SET from ~10% (2000) to ~75% (2013). The corresponding drop in multiple births went from ~25% (2000) to ~5% (2013), while delivery rates remained stable at 25-27% throughout the 2000s, demonstrating that SET policy did not reduce success rates.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 trend line in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 21. Summation text confirms approximately 75%.

facts.claim: In Sweden, the introduction of single embryo transfer (SET) as standard practice dramatically reduced the IVF multiple birth rate from approximately 25% in 2000 to approximately 5% by 2013, while delivery rates remained stable at 25-27% per fresh cycle (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Figure 14, page 21)
facts.body: Figure 14 shows three trend lines: SET rate rose from ~10% (2000) to ~75% (2013), multiple birth rate fell from ~25% (2000) to ~5% (2013), and delivery rate remained flat at 25-27%. This demonstrates that mandatory SET policy achieved its safety goal without sacrificing effectiveness. The sharpest changes occurred 2001-2004 following the 2003 National Board of Health and Welfare regulation mandating SET as routine.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 trend lines in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, page 21. Summary text confirms the narrative.

facts.claim: A total of 18,142 IVF treatments were started in Sweden in 2013, including 17,134 with own gametes, 453 oocyte donation cycles, 555 sperm donation cycles, and 650 donor inseminations (Q-IVF Annual Report 2015, Tables 1a-1b, pages 6-7)
facts.body: Own gametes comprised 11,267 fresh IVF cycles and 5,867 freeze cycles. Donated gametes were only available at public university clinics and one private clinic (IVF-kliniken Umea). All 16 IVF clinics in Sweden reported to Q-IVF.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Tables 1a and 1b in qivf-2015-swe.pdf, pages 6-7. Column sums cross-checked.

facts.claim: Over 70% of women undergoing IVF treatment in Sweden in 2014 were younger than 38 years old, with the largest group aged 30-34 (33.69%) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 3, page 10)
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 3, page 10 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data labels visible on pie chart.

facts.claim: The clinical pregnancy rate per started fresh IVF cycle for women aged 25-29 was 33% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 7, page 12)
facts.body: Clinical pregnancy = pregnancy with a gestational sac visible on ultrasound. Own gametes only. The rate declined with age: 31% (<25), 33% (25-29), 30% (30-34), 27% (35-37), 22% (38-39), 16% (40-41), 9% (>41).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 7, page 12 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data label shows 33%.

facts.claim: Sweden had 16 IVF clinics in 2014, comprising 6 public and 10 private clinics, all with 100% reporting to the Q-IVF registry (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Introduction, page 4)
facts.body: All clinics report annually to Q-IVF, with treatment data from two years prior to allow inclusion of delivery outcomes. Only university clinics (and the private Umea clinic with a teaching mandate) were permitted to perform gamete donation with IVF.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Introduction text, page 4 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged 25-29 was 29% in Sweden in 2014, the highest of any age group (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks. Own gametes only. The rate declined with increasing age: 28% (<25), 29% (25-29), 25% (30-34), 22% (35-37), 16% (38-39), 10% (40-41), 5% (>41).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data label clearly shows 29%.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged 30-34 was 25% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Own gametes, fresh IVF. Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12. Data label shows 25%.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged 35-37 was 22% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Own gametes, fresh IVF. Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12. Data label shows 22%.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged 38-39 was 16% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Own gametes, fresh IVF. Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12. Data label shows 16%.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged 40-41 was 10% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Own gametes, fresh IVF. Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12. Data label shows 10%.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started fresh cycle for women aged over 41 was 5% in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 8, page 12)
facts.body: Own gametes, fresh IVF. Delivery defined as birth of live or dead child after 22 complete pregnancy weeks. Lowest success rate of any age group.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 12. Data label shows 5%.

facts.claim: The delivery rate per embryo transfer for IVF with donated oocytes was 28% for fresh cycles and 20% for frozen cycles in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 12, page 14)
facts.body: Only university clinics (and the private Umea clinic) were permitted to perform IVF with donated gametes in 2014. Seven clinics reported donation activity.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 14 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data labels visible.

facts.claim: The delivery rate per embryo transfer for IVF with donated sperm was 34% for fresh cycles in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 12, page 14)
facts.body: Frozen cycle delivery rate with donated sperm was 29%. AID (donor insemination) delivery rate was 16%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 14 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data labels visible.

facts.claim: IVF-conceived children represented 3.8% of all children born in Sweden in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Summary, page 10)
facts.body: Based on 4,341 IVF babies out of all births in Sweden in 2014. IVF has been clinically established in Sweden for over 20 years with approximately 19,000 treatments per year at this time.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Summary box on page 10 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: Sweden reduced its IVF multiple birth rate for fresh embryos from over 30% to below 5% over a 25-year period through adoption of single embryo transfer, the lowest rate in the world (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Summary, page 13)
facts.body: The 2016 report marked the first year the multiple birth rate dropped below 5% (4.7% fresh). This decline is attributed to Sweden leading the world in SET adoption, which reached 80% for fresh cycles in 2014. Twins are the main component; triplet rates are near zero.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Summary box on page 13 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The total multiple birth rate after fresh IVF cycles in Sweden was 4.7% in 2014, dropping below 5% for the first time in the registry history (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, comment below Figure 10, page 13)
facts.body: This represents a decline from over 30% to under 5% over a 25-year period for fresh embryos, driven by Sweden leading the world in single embryo transfer adoption. The rate is somewhat higher in older age groups because they more frequently receive two embryos.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against comment text below Figure 10, page 13: "Den totala flerbordsfrekvensen efter farsk cykel var 4,7%"

facts.claim: The total multiple birth rate after frozen embryo transfer cycles in Sweden was 3.0% in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, comment below Figure 10, page 13)
facts.body: Lower than the fresh cycle rate (4.7%) due to the higher SET rate in frozen cycles (94% vs 80%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against comment text below Figure 10, page 13: "...och efter fryst cykel 3,0%"

facts.claim: 92% of IVF patients in Sweden agreed or strongly agreed that they received the best possible medical care in the 2015 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey (N=2,905), up from 90% in 2013 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 18, page 19)
facts.body: The IVF-KUPP survey was administered at all 16 Swedish IVF clinics (public and private). Five domains measured: Medical Care (92%), Accessibility (82%), Information (85%), Responsiveness (92%), and Participation (70%). All domains improved from 2013.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 18, page 19 and per-clinic charts pages 20-22 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for fresh IVF cycles in Sweden was 80% in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 6, page 11)
facts.body: SET rate increased from 77% in 2013. Sweden has the highest SET rates in the world. The remaining 20% were double embryo transfers (DET).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 6, page 11 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data label clearly shows 80%.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for frozen embryo transfer cycles in Sweden was 94% in 2014 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 6, page 11)
facts.body: SET rate for frozen cycles increased from 92.2% in 2013. Only 6% of frozen transfers involved two embryos.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 6, page 11 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Data label clearly shows 94%.

facts.claim: A total of 18,035 IVF cycles with own gametes were started in Sweden in 2014, comprising 11,975 fresh cycles and 6,060 frozen embryo transfer cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Figure 1 text, page 9)
facts.body: The total includes treatments at all 16 Swedish IVF clinics (6 public, 10 private) with 100% registry participation. This represented an increase from 17,239 cycles in 2013. Fresh cycles include both standard IVF and ICSI.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 1 text on page 9 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf. Clinic-level sums (11,975 fresh + 6,060 frozen = 18,035) match stated total.

facts.claim: IVF treatments in Sweden in 2014 resulted in 4,341 babies born, representing 3.8% of all children born in Sweden that year (Q-IVF Annual Report 2016, Summary box, page 10)
facts.body: This figure includes children born from all types of assisted reproduction: fresh IVF, frozen embryo transfer, donated gametes, and AID. The denominator is all births in Sweden in 2014.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Summary box on page 10 of qivf-2016-swe.pdf: "Detta resulterade i 4 341 fodda barn, vilket motsvarar 3,8% av alla barn som foddes i Sverige 2014."

facts.claim: The delivery rate per insemination with donated sperm (AID) in Sweden was 14.5% (2012), 17.2% (2013), 16.6% (2014), and 15.1% (2015) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 20)
facts.body: AID delivery rates over 4 years showing relatively stable performance around 15-17%. The 2015 figure of 15.1% is the lowest in the series but within normal variation.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 20 data labels (page 19) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, 959 inseminations with donated sperm (AID) were performed, with a delivery rate of 15.1% per insemination (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figures 19-20)
facts.body: AID treatments: Public 790 + Private 169 = 959. Delivery rate trend: 14.5% (2012), 17.2% (2013), 16.6% (2014), 15.1% (2015). In 2015, several private clinics received IVO permission for AID for the first time.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 19 (page 18) and Figure 20 (page 19) data labels in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, day 2-3 embryo transfer remained dominant for fresh IVF cycles (79-82% across age groups), while freeze cycles had higher blastocyst (day 5-6) transfer rates (25-39%) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 8)
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8 data labels (page 11) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: Sweden had 17 IVF clinics operating in 2015: 6 public and 11 private, with 100% participation in the Q-IVF registry (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Introduction)
facts.body: The report states: '6 offentliga och 11 privata'. Q-IVF has had 100% registry participation since its inception in 2007, making it one of the most complete IVF registries in the world with over 160,000 treatments registered.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Introduction (page 2) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, there were 94 deliveries from IVF with donated oocytes (40 from fresh cycles, 54 from frozen cycles) across all age groups (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 4)
facts.body: Deliveries by recipient age (Fresh/Frozen/Total): <30: 7/4/11, 30-35: 8/18/26, 36-37: 7/4/11, 38-39: 10/15/25, 40-41: 5/12/17, >42: 3/1/4. The donor's age, not the recipient's, is the primary factor affecting outcomes with donated oocytes.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4 (page 18) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf. Cross-check sum: 7+8+7+10+5+3=40 fresh, 4+18+4+15+12+1=54 frozen, 40+54=94 total.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, there were 184 deliveries from IVF with donated sperm (109 from fresh cycles, 75 from frozen cycles) across all age groups (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 5)
facts.body: Deliveries by age (Fresh/Frozen/Total): <30: 28/13/41, 30-35: 49/36/85, 36-37: 16/5/21, 38-39: 16/16/32, 40-41: 0/5/5. No fresh donation cycles performed after age 40.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 5 (page 18) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf. Cross-check sum: 28+49+16+16=109 fresh, 13+36+5+16+5=75 frozen, 109+75=184 total.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, 466 IVF cycles used donated sperm and 529 cycles used donated oocytes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 15)
facts.body: Donated sperm: 202 fresh + 264 frozen = 466. Donated oocytes: 342 fresh + 187 frozen = 529. Only university clinics (and one private clinic in Umea with teaching mandate) were permitted to perform IVF with donated gametes in 2015.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 15 data labels (page 16) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, 11,896 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles and 6,087 frozen embryo transfer cycles were started with own gametes (total 17,983 cycles) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 1)
facts.body: Public clinics: 4,467 fresh + 2,605 frozen = 7,072. Private clinics: 7,429 fresh + 3,482 frozen = 10,911. Some treatments at private clinics are publicly funded but cannot be distinguished in the registry.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 1 data labels (page 6) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf. Cross-check: 4467+7429=11896, 2605+3482=6087.

facts.claim: There were 2,506 deliveries from fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2015: 2,116 after SET and 390 after DET (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 3)
The IVF live birth rate per embryo transfer for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2015 ranged from 37.8% (<30 years) to 8.6% (>=42 years) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 10)
facts.body: Delivery per embryo transfer, fresh IVF/ICSI cycle, own gametes: <30: 37.8%, 30-35: 33.3%, 36-37: 28.7%, 38-39: 20.7%, 40-41: 17.1%, >=42: 8.6%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 data labels (page 13) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate was 23% per started cycle for fresh IVF with own gametes (all ages) in Sweden in 2015 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 9)
facts.body: Delivery rate per started cycle, all ages, fresh IVF with own gametes. By age: <30: 28%, 30-35: 26%, 36-37: 22%, 38-39: 16%, 40-41: 13%, >=42: 7%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 9 data labels (page 12) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per started cycle for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2015 by age: <30: 28%, 30-35: 26%, 36-37: 22%, 38-39: 16%, 40-41: 13%, >=42: 7% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 9)
facts.body: Woman's age is the most important factor for the chance of having a child. At higher ages, both egg quantity and quality deteriorate rapidly. Approximately one quarter of all women starting an IVF cycle have a child.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 9 data labels (page 12) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate per embryo transfer for frozen embryo transfer (FET) with own gametes in Sweden in 2015 ranged from 32.1% (<30 years) to 17.8% (>=42 years) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 10)
facts.body: Delivery per embryo transfer, freeze cycle, own gametes: <30: 32.1%, 30-35: 30.5%, 36-37: 29.0%, 38-39: 22.7%, 40-41: 20.5%, >=42: 17.8%. Higher >=42 frozen rates vs fresh are partly because frozen embryos may have been created when the woman was younger.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 data labels (page 13) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate after IVF in Sweden in 2015 was 4.5% for fresh cycles (4.5% twins, 0% triplets) and 3.0% for frozen cycles (2.9% twins, 0.1% triplets) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 2)
facts.body: The low multiple birth rate is attributed to the high SET rate (82-96%). The closing section states the rate is 'under five percent' nationally. Sweden is noted as a world leader in reducing multiple birth rates through single embryo transfer policies.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 2 (page 10) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, 305 oocyte freezing cycles were performed: 112 for oncological reasons and 192 for non-medical reasons (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 1 and Figure 23)
facts.body: This was the first year Q-IVF reported oocyte freezing cycles separately. The total from Table 1 (305) does not perfectly match the sum of Figure 23 categories at national level (112+192=304), likely due to rounding or incomplete categorization. The report notes that most frozen oocytes have not yet been thawed for use, so pregnancy outcomes cannot be reported.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1 (page 7) and Figure 23 (page 21) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: Patient satisfaction with Swedish IVF clinics in 2016 (IVF-KUPP survey, n=2,496): 92% for medical care, 91% for approach, 83% for information, 78% for accessibility, 66% for participation (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 26)
facts.body: The IVF-KUPP (Quality from the Patient's Perspective) is a scientifically validated survey administered at all Swedish IVF clinics. The 2016 survey was the third administration (after 2013 and 2015). Results represent the proportion agreeing completely or largely with questions in 5 domains. N=2,496 respondents (1,466 women, 1,030 men).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 26 data labels (page 25) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2015, the delivery rate per embryo transfer for fresh IVF with own gametes was 30% after SET and 25% after DET (all ages combined) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 11)
facts.body: SET vs DET delivery rates by age: <30: 38% vs 35%, 30-35: 34% vs 30%, 36-37: 27% vs 37%, 38-39: 22% vs 20%, 40-41: 16% vs 19%, >=42: 7% vs 11%. The report notes that SET and DET rates are not directly comparable as the decision to transfer one or two embryos varies with the individual patient's prognosis.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 11 data labels (page 13) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate in Sweden in 2015 was 82% for fresh IVF cycles and 96% for frozen embryo transfer cycles with own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Figure 7)
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 7 data labels (page 10) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: Approximately 19,244 IVF and related fertility treatments were started in Sweden in 2015, across 17 clinics (6 public, 11 private), with 100% registry participation (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Table 1)
facts.body: Sum of Table 1: Fresh IVF/ICSI 11,671 + Freeze cycles 6,162 + Egg donation 146 + Own oocyte freeze 305 + Insemination (AID) 960 = 19,244. The closing section states 'approximately 19,000 treatments'. This was the 10th Q-IVF annual report.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1 (page 7) and Introduction (page 2) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf. Cross-checked sum: 11671+6162+146+305+960=19244.

facts.claim: The number of IVF treatments in Sweden grew from approximately 3,000 in 1992 to approximately 19,000 in 2015, with almost 4% of all children born in Sweden conceived through IVF (Q-IVF Annual Report 2017, Introduction)
facts.body: The text states: 'Number and treatments have grown from about 3,000 years in 1992 to about 19,000 today. Almost four percent of all children born in Sweden have been born through an IVF treatment.' The registry contained over 160,000 treatments total as of 2017.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Introduction (page 5) in qivf-2017-swe.pdf.

facts.claim: 1,452 donor inseminations (AID) were performed in Sweden in 2016, with a delivery rate of 16.8% per insemination (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figures 19-20)
facts.body: Figure 19: 867 public + 585 private = 1,452 total AID. Figure 20: 16.8% delivery per insemination in 2016. Approximately 500 more than the previous year due to law change (April 2016) allowing single women assisted reproduction and more private clinics receiving IVO permission.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 19 (page 25) and Figure 20 (page 25) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 74% of fresh IVF embryo transfers and 81% of freeze cycle transfers were at the blastocyst stage (day 5-6) in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figures 8a-8b)
facts.body: Figure 8a: Fresh IVF all ages day 5-6 = 74%, day 2-3 = 25%. Figure 8b: Freeze cycles all ages day 5-6 = 81%, day 2-3 = 18%. Higher blastocyst rate in freeze cycles due to vitrification technique for blastocysts.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figures 8a-8b (page 15) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 17 IVF clinics were operating in Sweden in 2016: 6 public and 11 private, with 100% registry participation in Q-IVF (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Introduction)
facts.body: Introduction, page 7. The registry held nearly 190,000 treatment cycles at the time of the 2018 report. IVF with donated gametes was restricted to university clinics with teaching mandates, plus one private clinic (Livio Umea) with a special permit.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Introduction (page 7) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 162 complications were reported among 12,174 fresh IVF cycles in Sweden in 2016, a rate of 1.3% among all cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 3)
facts.body: Table 3, page 13. 162 complications reported yes, 10,789 reported no, 1,223 not reported. Total 12,174 cycles. Complication reporting started in 2014 and was 'still not complete'. Among reported cycles: 162/(162+10,789) = 1.48%. Most common complication is OHSS; bleeding, infection, and blood clots are 'extremely rare'.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 3 (page 13) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 4,469 deliveries resulted from IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2016: 2,642 from fresh cycles and 1,827 from freeze cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 1)
facts.body: Table 1, page 9. Fresh deliveries: 1,338 St.IVF + 1,204 ICSI ejac + 89 ICSI testicular + 11 other = 2,642. Frozen: 1,827. Own gametes only, PGD excluded.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1 (page 9) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 342 IVF cycles with donated oocytes were started in Sweden in 2016: 189 fresh and 153 freeze cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 15)
facts.body: Figure 15, page 21. Public: 150 fresh + 141 frozen = 291. Private: 39 fresh + 12 frozen = 51. Delivery per ET: 28% fresh all ages, 23% frozen all ages (Figure 18). Donor's age matters more than recipient's age for outcomes.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 15 (page 21) and Figure 18 (page 23) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 758 IVF cycles with donated sperm were started in Sweden in 2016: 447 fresh and 311 freeze cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 15)
facts.body: Figure 15, page 21. Public: 378 fresh + 282 frozen = 660. Private: 69 fresh + 29 frozen = 98. Delivery per ET: 35% fresh all ages, 33% frozen all ages (Figure 17).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 15 (page 21) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 11,954 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles with own gametes (excluding PGD) were started in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 1)
facts.body: Table 1, page 9. Breakdown: St.IVF 6,024 + Total ICSI 5,930 (ejaculated 5,532 + epididymal/testicular 398). Own gametes only, PGD excluded.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1 (page 9) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 5,182 children (4.3% of all births in Sweden) were born through IVF treatment in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Introduction)
facts.body: Introduction, page 7. This figure comes from the Swedish Medical Birth Register cross-reference, not Q-IVF alone. Includes all IVF births (own and donated gametes).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Introduction (page 7) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 31.2% per embryo transfer for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 10)
facts.body: Figure 10, page 16. All ages, fresh IVF. Age breakdown per ET: <30: 38.1%, 30-35: 32.6%, 36-37: 31.4%, 38-39: 27.7%, 40-41: 23.3%, >42: 14.0%. Note: Table 1 calculation (2,642/9,144) gives 28.9%, suggesting Figure 10 uses a different denominator.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 (page 16) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 22% per started cycle for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 9)
facts.body: Figure 9, page 16. All ages. Cross-check: Table 1 fresh deliveries (2,642) / started cycles (11,954) = 22.1%. Age breakdown: <30: 28%, 30-35: 26%, 36-37: 22%, 38-39: 16%, 40-41: 13%, >=42: 6%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 9 (page 16) and Table 1 (page 9) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 28.6% per embryo transfer for freeze cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 10)
facts.body: Figure 10, page 16. All ages, freeze cycles. Age breakdown per ET: <30: 36.0%, 30-35: 33.2%, 36-37: 26.5%, 38-39: 21.1%, 40-41: 14.7%, >42: 8.6%. Higher delivery rates in older age groups for FET vs fresh may reflect embryos frozen when woman was younger.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 (page 16) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 4,531 live born children resulted from IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2016: 2,679 from fresh cycles and 1,852 from freeze cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 1)
facts.body: Table 1, page 9. Fresh: 1,362 St.IVF + 1,267 ICSI + 50 unknown = 2,679. Frozen: 1,852. Includes singletons (2,600+1,801), twins (77+49), and triplets (2+2). 5,182 total IVF children (4.3% of all births) reported from Medical Birth Register cross-reference.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1 (page 9) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate after IVF was 2.8% for twins and 0.1% for triplets in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 4)
facts.body: Table 4, page 14. Breakdown: Fresh IVF twins 3.06%, triplets 0.08%; Freeze cycle twins 2.54%, triplets 0.11%; Total twins 2.8%, triplets 0.1%. Narrative confirms the multiple birth rate is 'now under 3%' thanks to SET policy.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4 (page 14) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 389 oocyte freezing cycles were performed in Sweden in 2016: 102 for oncological indications and 287 for non-medical reasons (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 23)
facts.body: Figure 23, page 28. Public: 98 oncological + 26 non-medical = 124. Private: 4 oncological + 261 non-medical = 265. Non-medical oocyte freezing nearly 100 more than previous year. Most common in ages 30-39. Vitrification technique made oocyte freezing more effective.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 23 (page 28) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: IVF patient satisfaction in Sweden in the 2018 IVF-KUPP survey was 91% for medical care, 85% for information, and 92% for responsiveness (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 26)
facts.body: Figure 26, page 36. 4th IVF-KUPP survey. Five domains: Medical care 91%, Accessibility 83%, Information 85%, Responsiveness 92%, Participation 69%. n=2,900 responses. Women response rate 70.7%, partners 49.5%. All five domains stable or improving vs previous surveys (2013-2016).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 26 (page 36) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate was 82.2% for fresh IVF cycles in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 6a)
facts.body: Figure 6a, page 14. Own gametes. DET rate was 17.7%. SET rate increased from 69.6% in 2007 to 82.2% in 2016. The narrative states this trend has 'dramatically reduced' the multiple birth rate.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 6a (page 14) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate was 97.5% for freeze cycles in Sweden in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 7b)
facts.body: Figure 7b, page 14. Own gametes. DET rate was only 2.5%. SET rate for freeze cycles increased from 76.7% in 2007 to 97.5% in 2016.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 7b (page 14) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: A total of 21,145 assisted reproduction treatments were started in Sweden in 2016, including 12,810 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles, 6,297 freeze cycles, 189 egg donor cycles, 397 oocyte freezing cycles, and 1,452 donor inseminations (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Table 2)
facts.body: Sum of all treatment types from Table 2, page 11. Fresh IVF/ICSI and freeze cycle counts include IVF with donated gametes (egg/sperm). Concluding remarks confirm 'more than 21,000 treatments'. 17 clinics (6 public, 11 private) with 100% registry participation.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 2 (page 11) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: Of 12,810 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles started in Sweden in 2016 (all gamete types), 11,851 reached oocyte retrieval, 9,754 reached embryo transfer, 3,374 achieved clinical pregnancy, and 2,809 resulted in delivery (Q-IVF Annual Report 2018, Figure 4)
facts.body: Figure 4, page 12. Treatment attrition: 7.5% did not reach OPU, 17.7% of OPUs did not reach ET, 65.4% of ETs did not result in clinical pregnancy, 16.7% of clinical pregnancies did not result in delivery. Overall: 21.9% of started cycles resulted in delivery.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 4 (page 12) in qivf-2018-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The delivery rate per donor insemination (AID) was 15.5% per insemination in Sweden in 2017, with 282 deliveries from 1,817 inseminations (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Tables 9-10).
facts.body: 2,170 AID cycles started, 1,817 inseminations performed. Rate varied by age: 17.9% (<30), 19.0% (30-35), 11.5% (36-37), 10.6% (38-39), 5.1% (40-41), 4.5% (42+). AID treatments surged from 959 (2015) to 2,170 (2017) after single women were permitted treatment.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Tables 9-10 (pages 27-28) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 88% of frozen embryo transfers in Sweden in 2017 used blastocyst-stage embryos (day 5-6), compared to 31% for fresh IVF cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figures 8a-8b).
facts.body: Figure 8a (fresh): day 5-6 = 31%. Figure 8b (frozen): day 5-6 = 88%. The high blastocyst rate in frozen cycles reflects better vitrification outcomes.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figures 8a and 8b, page 15 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: There were 18 IVF clinics operating in Sweden in 2017 (6 public and 12 private), all reporting to the Q-IVF registry with 100% participation (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, page 3).
facts.body: Introduction, page 3. The 6 public clinics were at university hospitals. Of the 12 private clinics, Nordic Stockholm had minimal activity (5 fresh, 0 frozen cycles).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 3 and Figure 2 (page 10) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The reported complication rate for fresh IVF was 1.5% in Sweden in 2017, including 60 hospitalized OHSS cases (0.5%), 10 bleeding complications, 9 infections, and 3 ovarian torsions from 11,659 fresh treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, page 13).
facts.body: Table 3: 173 complications reported, 9,101 no complications, 2,385 not reported (20.5% missing data). Complication reporting started in 2014 and was still incomplete.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 3 and page 13 narrative in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 341 IVF treatment cycles with donated oocytes were started in Sweden in 2017 (145 fresh + 196 frozen), with a delivery rate of approximately 27% per fresh ET and 29% per frozen ET (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Table 6, Figure 20).
facts.body: Table 6: Fresh 82 ETs, 22 deliveries (26.8%). Frozen 185 ETs, 54 deliveries (29.2%). Small sample sizes make percentages unreliable.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 6 (page 22) and Figure 20 (page 25) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 892 IVF treatment cycles with donated sperm were started in Sweden in 2017 (535 fresh + 357 frozen), with a delivery rate of 35% per embryo transfer for both fresh and frozen cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Table 6, Figure 19).
facts.body: Table 6 (page 22): Fresh 535 started, 433 ETs, 153 deliveries. Frozen 357 started, 342 ETs, 120 deliveries. Figure 19 all-ages: 35% for both.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 6 (page 22) and Figure 19 (page 24) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate for women aged 38-39 was 17.0% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017, compared to 30.3% for frozen embryo transfer in the same age group (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 10).
facts.body: The higher frozen ET rate (30.3% vs 17.0%) in the 38-39 age group reflects that embryos may have been created and frozen when the woman was younger.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10, page 16 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate for women aged 42 and older was 10.4% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 10).
facts.body: Oldest age group. Fresh IVF delivery rate per ET drops dramatically with age. At 42+, only about 1 in 10 embryo transfers results in delivery.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10, page 16 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate for women under 30 was 39.8% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 10).
facts.body: Youngest age group. Fresh IVF delivery rate 39.8% per ET vs 36.2% for frozen ET. Rate declined sharply with age: 34.9% (30-35), 27.6% (36-37), 17.0% (38-39), 16.1% (40-41), 10.4% (42+).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10, page 16 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 29.3% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 10).
facts.body: Cross-check: 2,516 deliveries / 8,576 embryo transfers = 29.3%. Confirmed in concluding section page 39. Rate was stable compared to previous years.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 (page 16), Table 1 (page 9), and concluding section (page 39) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 22% per started cycle for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 9).
facts.body: Cross-check: 2,516 deliveries / 11,659 started cycles = 21.6%, rounded to 22% in Figure 9. Narrative on page 12 confirms approximately one in five started treatments resulted in birth.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 9 (page 16), Table 1 (page 9), and page 12 narrative in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 32.6% per embryo transfer for frozen embryo transfer cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 10).
facts.body: Cross-check: 2,194 deliveries / 6,718 frozen embryo transfers = 32.7%. Figure 10 shows 32.6% (minor rounding difference). FET delivery rates had been increasing due to improved vitrification and blastocyst culture.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10 (page 16) and Table 1 (page 9) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 5,137 children born through IVF treatment in Sweden in 2017 represented 4.3% of all children born in Sweden that year (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, page 7).
facts.body: Page 7 narrative. This includes IVF with own and donated gametes but excludes AID insemination children (291 additional).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 7 narrative in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: A total of 5,428 children were born alive after all forms of assisted reproduction in Sweden in 2017, with 4,787 from IVF with own gametes, 350 from IVF with donated gametes, and 291 from donor insemination (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Tables 1, 6, 9).
facts.body: Page 7 narrative states 5,137 (4.3%) children born through IVF treatment; this includes only IVF, not AID. Adding AID live-born (291) gives 5,428 total.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Tables 1 (page 9), 6 (page 22), 9 (page 27) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate after IVF was 3.2% overall in Sweden in 2017, with 4.0% twins and 0.1% triplets after fresh IVF, and 2.3% twins after frozen cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Table 4).
facts.body: Table 4, page 14. Very low multiple birth rate by international standards, directly attributable to high SET rates (83.7% fresh, 98.2% frozen).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4, page 14 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: 361 oocyte freezing procedures were performed in Sweden in 2017, including 102 for oncological reasons, 250 for non-medical (social) reasons, and 9 for other medical reasons (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, page 30).
facts.body: Total decreased slightly from 2016. Oncological freezing (102) stable. Non-medical freezing (250) declined somewhat. Few frozen oocytes had yet been thawed and used.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 30 narrative and Table 2 (page 11) in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate was 83.7% for fresh IVF cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 7a).
facts.body: Trend from Figure 7a: SET rate rose from 69.6% (2007) to 83.7% (2017) for fresh IVF. DET rate declined correspondingly from 30.0% to 16.2%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 7a, page 14 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate was 98.2% for frozen embryo transfer cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2017 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Figure 7b).
facts.body: Trend from Figure 7b: SET rate for frozen cycles rose from 76.7% (2007) to 98.2% (2017). Nearly all frozen embryo transfers used single embryo by 2017.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 7b, page 14 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: A total of 5,341 deliveries resulted from all assisted reproduction treatments in Sweden in 2017 (4,710 from IVF with own gametes, 349 from donated gametes, 282 from donor insemination) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Tables 1, 6, 9).
facts.body: Own gametes (Table 1): 2,516 fresh + 2,194 frozen = 4,710. Donated gametes (Table 6): 349. AID (Table 9): 282. Total: 5,341.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Tables 1, 6, 9 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: A total of 22,403 assisted reproduction treatments were started in Sweden in 2017, including 18,639 IVF cycles with own gametes, 1,233 IVF cycles with donated gametes, 2,170 donor inseminations, and 361 oocyte freezing procedures (Q-IVF Annual Report 2019, Table 2).
facts.body: Sum of all treatment types from Table 2 (page 11): own gametes fresh 11,659 + frozen 6,980 + oocyte donation 341 + sperm donation 892 + AID 2,170 + oocyte freezing 361 = 22,403. Treated across 18 clinics (6 public, 12 private). Registry is 100% complete for all clinics.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 2, page 11 in qivf-2019-swe.pdf

facts.claim: A total of 2,237 donor insemination (AID) cycles were started in Sweden in 2018, resulting in 340 deliveries and 343 live-born children, a delivery rate of 14.5% per insemination (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Table 8 and Figure 24).
facts.body: 2,174 inseminations were performed out of 2,237 started cycles. Delivery rates by age: <30: 19%, 30-35: 17.1%, 36-37: 11.8%, 38-39: 7.2%, 40-41: 3.4%. Single women comprised 761 AID cycles (34% of total AID).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 8 and Figure 24 in qivf-2020-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In 2018, Sweden had 18 IVF clinics (6 public and 12 private) all reporting to the Q-IVF national registry with near 100% coverage (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020).
facts.body: Q-IVF is one of the most complete national IVF registries in the world, with all public and private clinics participating. Over 240,000 treatment cycles registered since Q-IVF launched in 2007. Some private clinic treatments are state-funded through regional procurement.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 2 (Notes from head of registry) in qivf-2020-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate with donated oocytes was 36.3% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles and 24.2% per embryo transfer for freeze cycles in Sweden in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 21).
facts.body: For donated oocytes, the recipient woman's age does not significantly affect outcomes; the donor's age is more important. Donors are mostly younger women. Small sample sizes (102 fresh ET, 244 freeze ET) mean rates may be less reliable. Prior to 2019, oocyte donation was only allowed at university hospitals.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 21, page 26 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2018, for the first time freeze cycle live births (2,524) equaled fresh IVF live births (2,522) across all treatment types including donated gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 19).
facts.body: A landmark shift: freeze cycles matched fresh IVF in total live births for the first time in 2018. This trend reflects improved vitrification methods, extended culture to blastocyst stage, and growing freeze-all strategies. Figure 19 includes all treatments (own gametes + donated gametes + PGT).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 19, page 22 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate for women aged 40-41 was 12.9% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 14).
facts.body: Age 40-41 shows sharply reduced success compared to younger groups. Only 9.6% per started cycle (Figure 13).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate for women aged 42 and older was 9.3% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 14).
facts.body: Lowest age-group success rate. Only 6.9% per started cycle (Figure 13). Women 42+ comprised 4.3% of all fresh IVF patients (Figure 6).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate was 28.0% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2018, all ages combined (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 14).
facts.body: Denominator: 8,066 embryo transfers from fresh IVF/ICSI cycles with own gametes. Numerator: 2,255 deliveries. Concluding remarks on page 43 states 29.3% per embryo transfer, which may use a slightly different denominator. Figure 14 value (28.0%) used as primary.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate was 20.4% per started cycle for fresh IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2018, all ages combined (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 13).
facts.body: Denominator: 11,061 started fresh IVF/ICSI cycles with own gametes. Not all started cycles reach embryo transfer due to canceled stimulation, failed fertilization, freeze-all strategy, or other reasons.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 13, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate for women under 30 was 38.8% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 14).
facts.body: Highest age-group success rate for fresh IVF. Age is the most important factor determining IVF success, as oocyte quality and quantity decline with age.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate was 33.5% per embryo transfer for freeze cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2018, all ages combined (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 14).
facts.body: Denominator: 6,787 embryo transfers from freeze cycles with own gametes. Freeze cycle results have improved in recent years, largely due to extended culture to blastocyst stage and improved vitrification methods.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 19 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data label.

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate per delivery from IVF with own gametes was 2.54% in Sweden in 2018 (2.70% fresh, 2.37% freeze), with zero triplet deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Table 4).
facts.body: Among the lowest IVF multiple birth rates in the world. Several countries still have rates of 25-30%. Sweden achieved this through the one-embryo-transfer policy. Spontaneous conception multiple rate is approximately 1%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4, page 17 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT values.

facts.claim: There were 42 cases of OHSS requiring hospitalization out of 11,061 fresh IVF treatment cycles in Sweden in 2018, a rate of 0.38% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020).
facts.body: OHSS is the most common IVF complication. Moderate or severe forms occur in 1-5% of women. The 0.38% rate represents only severe cases requiring hospitalization. Additionally, 9 bleeding events and 11 infection events were reported. Total reported complications: 134 out of 11,061 cycles (1.21%), though 15% of treatments had no complication report filed.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against narrative text and Table 3 (complications), page 15 in qivf-2020-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2018, 448 oocyte freezing cycles were performed: 139 for oncologic indication, 283 for non-medical (social) reasons, and 26 for other medical reasons (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figures 25-26).
facts.body: Both oncologic and social oocyte freezing increased from prior years. Social egg freezing was predominantly at private clinics (250 of 283). Oncologic freezing was predominantly at public clinics (132 of 139). Results from thawed oocytes cannot yet be reported due to insufficient follow-up data.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figures 25 and 26, page 31 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: In the 2019 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey across all Swedish IVF clinics, 92% of patients rated medical care positively, 93% rated staff approach positively, 88% rated information positively, 84% rated availability positively, and 74% rated participation in decision-making positively (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 29).
facts.body: IVF-KUPP is a validated patient survey administered every 18 months across all Swedish IVF clinics. Fall 2019 survey: 4,855 questionnaires sent, 71% female response rate, 50% partner response rate. Results improved across all categories compared to prior surveys. Participation showed the largest gain (69% to 74%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 29, page 38 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2018, 330 PGT (Preimplantation Genetic Testing) cycles were started, resulting in 262 embryo transfers, 93 clinical pregnancies, and 83 deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 8).
facts.body: PGT delivery rate: 25.2% per started cycle, 31.7% per embryo transfer. PGT is performed at two clinics in Sweden (Gothenburg and Stockholm). Sweden allows PGT for known monogenic diseases and inherited chromosome abnormalities; PGT-A (aneuploidy screening) is only allowed in clinical trials.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8, page 14 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for fresh IVF cycles was 86.7% in Sweden in 2018, up from 69.6% in 2007 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 10a).
facts.body: Steady increase in SET adoption from 69.6% (2007) to 86.7% (2018) for fresh IVF. DET declined from 30.0% to 13.1% over the same period. Swedish policy mandates SET unless twin risk is judged low.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10a, page 16 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for freeze cycles was 98.2% in Sweden in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Figure 10b).
facts.body: Near-universal SET in freeze cycles, with DET at only 1.7%. The freeze cycle SET rate increased from 76.7% in 2007 to 98.2% in 2018.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10b, page 16 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. EXACT data labels.

facts.claim: A total of 4,528 deliveries resulted from 18,102 IVF treatments with own gametes in Sweden in 2018, comprising 2,255 fresh IVF deliveries and 2,273 freeze cycle deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Table 1).
facts.body: These 4,528 deliveries produced 4,591 live-born children (including twins). Fresh IVF: 2,193 singleton + 62 twin deliveries. Freeze: 2,219 singleton + 54 twin deliveries. Zero triplet deliveries in either category.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 10 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. Cross-check: singleton + twin + triplet sums match totals.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2018, 5,043 children were born as a result of IVF and 343 children through donor insemination, for a total of 5,386 children born through assisted reproduction (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020).
facts.body: Total of 5,386 children born via assisted reproduction in Sweden in 2018. IVF figure (5,043) includes all IVF modalities (own gametes, donated gametes, PGT). AID figure (343) covers donor insemination only.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 7 in qivf-2020-en.pdf.

facts.claim: A total of 18,102 IVF treatment cycles with own gametes were started in Sweden in 2018, comprising 11,061 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles and 7,041 freeze cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2020, Table 1).
facts.body: Reported by Q-IVF national registry covering all 18 Swedish IVF clinics (6 public, 12 private). Near 100% national coverage. Own gametes only; excludes donated gamete cycles, AID, and oocyte freezing.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 10 in qivf-2020-en.pdf. Cross-checked with Figure 3 (national totals match).

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, blastocyst (day 5-6) transfers constituted 35% of fresh IVF embryo transfers and 94% of frozen/thawed embryo transfers with own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figures 12a-12b, page 19).
facts.body: For fresh cycles, day 2-3 transfers still predominated at 65%. For frozen cycles, vitrification of blastocysts yields higher survival rates (97-98%) and better pregnancy rates than slow freezing of day 2-3 embryos. Concluding remarks state 98% of all transfers with frozen/thawed embryos are blastocysts (may include donated gametes). 90% of freeze cycles use day 5-6 culture.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figures 12a-12b page 19 and concluding remarks page 41 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: Among fresh IVF treatments initiated in Sweden in 2019, there were 60 cases of OHSS requiring hospitalization out of 10,421 oocyte retrievals (0.58%), along with 18 cases of bleeding and 10 cases of infection (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, page 16).
facts.body: Total reported complications: 180 out of 10,421 OPU (1.73%). However, 2,004 cases (19.2%) had no complication data reported. The overall complication rate is low. Infection risk after oocyte retrieval is reported as 0.3%. Major bleeding occurs in 1-2 per 1,000 OPU.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 16 narrative and Table 4 page 16 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The cumulative live birth rate per oocyte retrieval in Sweden increased from 27.0% in 2007 to 36.3% in 2017, including all fresh and frozen embryo transfers within one year after oocyte retrieval (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, page 24).
facts.body: Includes women not receiving any embryo transfer. If restricted to women receiving at least one ET, the cumulative LBR increased from 30.0% to 43.3%. The improvement is attributed to blastocyst culture and vitrification. Reference: Saket Z, Kallen K, Lundin K, Magnusson A, Bergh C. Hum Reprod Open. 2021;2021(3):hoab021. PMID: 34195386.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 24 narrative text and Figure 21 page 24 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 1,576 IVF cycles with donated sperm and 423 IVF cycles with donated oocytes were started, resulting in 411 and 95 deliveries respectively (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Table 6, page 26).
facts.body: Donated sperm: 1,035 fresh + 541 freeze = 1,576 started; 225+186 = 411 deliveries; 229+187 = 416 live born children. Donated oocytes: 159 fresh + 264 freeze = 423 started; 28+67 = 95 deliveries; 28+68 = 96 live born children. Private clinics allowed to offer donated gamete treatments from 2019.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 6 page 26 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 2,492 insemination with donated sperm (IUI-D) cycles were started, resulting in 323 deliveries and 328 live births. The delivery rate per insemination was 14.0% for all ages (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Table 9, page 30).
facts.body: Denominator for delivery rate is inseminations performed (2,305), not started cycles (2,492). Age-specific rates: <30 years 18.2%, 30-35 years 16.0%, 36-37 years 12.9%, 38-39 years 6.5%, 40-41 years 3.0%, >=42 years 7.5%. The >=42 rate of 7.5% is based on only 40 inseminations (3 deliveries), so unreliable.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 9 page 30, Figure 26 page 31, and Table 10 page 31 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, the fresh IVF delivery rate per embryo transfer by age group was: <30 years 39.0%, 30-35 years 35.3%, 36-37 years 25.7%, 38-39 years 20.2%, 40-41 years 15.7%, and 42+ years 5.6%, with own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 14, page 20).
facts.body: Age is the most important factor for IVF success. The delivery rate drops substantially after age 37 and is very low above 42. These rates are per embryo transfer, not per started cycle (which are lower due to cycle cancellations and freeze-all strategies).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 page 20 in qivf-2021-en.pdf. Data labels clearly visible.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 29.0% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2019 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 14, page 20).
facts.body: Denominator: 7,791 embryo transfers (fresh IVF/ICSI, own gametes, PGT excluded). Numerator: 2,260 deliveries. Cross-check: 2,260/7,791 = 29.0%. Page 14 text states 28% (rounded differently).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 page 20 and Table 1 page 11 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 20% per started fresh cycle with own gametes in Sweden in 2019 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 13, page 20).
facts.body: Denominator: 11,093 started fresh IVF/ICSI cycles (own gametes, PGT excluded). Cross-check: 2,260 deliveries / 11,093 started = 20.4%. Report rounds to 20%. Not all started cycles lead to ET -- about 20% of OPU do not proceed to fresh ET (freeze-all, fertilization failure, etc.).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 13 page 20 and page 14 text in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, the frozen/thawed IVF delivery rate per embryo transfer by age group was: <30 years 40.1%, 30-35 years 38.7%, 36-37 years 33.2%, 38-39 years 30.7%, 40-41 years 20.2%, and 42+ years 15.9%, with own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 14, page 20).
facts.body: Frozen cycle rates are higher than fresh at older ages because embryos may have been frozen when the woman was younger. This is an important caveat when interpreting frozen cycle success rates by age. The improvement in frozen cycle outcomes is associated with blastocyst culture and vitrification.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 page 20 in qivf-2021-en.pdf. Data labels clearly visible.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate was 35.0% per embryo transfer for frozen/thawed cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2019 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 14, page 20).
facts.body: Denominator: 7,091 embryo transfers (frozen/thawed cycles, own gametes). Numerator: 2,482 deliveries. Cross-check: 2,482/7,091 = 35.0%. Frozen cycle results have improved in recent years due to blastocyst culture and vitrification.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14 page 20 and Table 1 page 11 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate per delivery for fresh IVF/ICSI cycles in Sweden in 2019 was 2.85% (2.81% twins + 0.04% triplets), according to the Q-IVF Annual Report 2021 (Figure 11a, page 18).
facts.body: Among the lowest multiple birth rates in the world for IVF. Several other countries had multiple pregnancy rates of 25-30%. Cross-check: Table 5 shows 64 twin + 1 triplet = 65 multiples out of 2,260 fresh deliveries = 2.88%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 11a page 18 and Table 5 page 18 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate per delivery for frozen/thawed IVF cycles in Sweden in 2019 was 1.93% (1.89% twins + 0.04% triplets), according to the Q-IVF Annual Report 2021 (Figure 11a, page 18).
facts.body: Frozen cycle multiple birth rate is lower than fresh due to even higher SET rates (99% vs 88%). Cross-check: Table 5 shows 48 twin + 1 triplet = 49 multiples out of 2,482 freeze deliveries = 1.97%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 11a page 18 and Table 5 page 18 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 591 oocyte freezing cycles were performed (187 oncologic, 400 non-medical, 39 other medical), a 40% increase from 421 in 2018 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figures 27-28, page 33).
facts.body: Public clinics: 224 cycles. Private clinics: 367 cycles. Non-medical (social) freezing is predominantly performed at private clinics (365 of 400). Oncologic freezing is predominantly at public clinics (170 of 187). Oocyte freezing is increasing mainly among women younger than 39 years.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 27 and Figure 28 page 33 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In the 2019 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey across all Swedish IVF clinics, satisfaction rates were: medical care 92%, availability 84%, information 88%, approach from staff 93%, and participation 74% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 33, page 40).
facts.body: The IVF-KUPP survey was performed for the fifth time in fall 2019 (3 months). 4,855 questionnaires sent to women and partners. Response rate: 71% women, 50% partners. 57% first-time IVF patients. Participation showed the largest improvement (69% to 74% from 2018). Information also improved (84% to 88%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 33 page 40 and page 39 narrative in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 335 PGT (preimplantation genetic testing) fresh IVF cycles were started, resulting in 76 deliveries after both fresh and frozen transfers combined (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 8, page 15).
facts.body: PGT performed at two clinics (Gothenburg and Stockholm). Treatment funnel: 335 started -> 320 OPU -> 228 ET (fresh+freeze) -> 89 clinical pregnancies -> 76 deliveries. Nearly 500 babies born from PGT in Sweden since 1996. PGT-A (aneuploidy screening) only allowed in clinical trials.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 8 page 15 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for fresh IVF cycles in Sweden reached 88% in 2019, up from 70% in 2007 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 10a, page 17).
facts.body: Autologous gametes. DET (double embryo transfer) decreased correspondingly from 30% to 12%. The Swedish policy since 2003 is to transfer one embryo as a rule, with DET allowed only when twin risk is estimated to be low.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10a page 17 in qivf-2021-en.pdf. Both 2007 and 2019 data labels clearly visible.

facts.claim: The single embryo transfer (SET) rate for frozen/thawed IVF cycles in Sweden reached 99% in 2019, up from 77% in 2007 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 10b, page 17).
facts.body: Autologous gametes. DET decreased from 23% (2007) to 1% (2019). Frozen cycles achieved near-universal SET earlier than fresh cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10b page 17 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 982 fresh IVF/ICSI treatments and 597 IUI-D treatments were started in single women (Q-IVF Annual Report 2021, Figure 24, page 30).
facts.body: Public: 269 IVF + 276 IUI-D. Private: 706 IVF + 328 IUI-D. National total: 982 IVF + 597 IUI-D = 1,579 treatments in single women. Single women became eligible for assisted reproduction in Sweden in 2016.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 24 page 30 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2019, 18,407 IVF treatment cycles were started using the patients own gametes (11,093 fresh and 7,314 frozen embryo transfer cycles), according to the Q-IVF Annual Report 2021 (Table 1, page 11).
facts.body: Own gametes (autologous) IVF/ICSI only, PGT excluded. Breakdown by method: St.IVF 5,668 + ICSI Ejaculated 5,135 + ICSI Epididymis/Testicle 290 = 11,093 fresh. Denominator: started cycles (not aspirations or transfers).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2021-en.pdf. Cross-check: Figure 3 national bar = 11,093 fresh + 7,314 freeze.

facts.claim: IVF treatments with own gametes in Sweden in 2019 resulted in 4,742 deliveries and 4,819 live born children, according to the Q-IVF Annual Report 2021 (Table 1, page 11).
facts.body: Includes both fresh IVF/ICSI (2,260 deliveries, 2,320 children) and frozen embryo transfers (2,482 deliveries, 2,499 children). Own gametes only, PGT excluded. Singleton deliveries: 4,632. Twin deliveries: 108. Triplet deliveries: 2.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2021-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, blastocysts constituted 99% of all frozen-thawed embryo transfers. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Concluding Remarks page 47.
facts.body: Concluding Remarks explicitly states blastocysts now constitute 99% of all transfers with cryopreserved embryos. This is the preferred authoritative figure over the visual chart estimate from Figure 36, which showed approximately 93% estimated from chart. The near-universal use of blastocyst-stage embryos in frozen cycles reflects the standard of extended culture to day 5-6 before vitrification, enabling better embryo selection. For fresh cycles, Figure 12 reports 67.7% blastocyst transfers among all fresh IVF embryo transfers.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Concluding Remarks, page 47 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: blastocysts now constitutes 99% of all transfers with cryopreserved embryos. Preferred over Figure 36 visual estimate of approximately 93%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, IVF treatments were performed at 20 clinics: 6 public and 14 private. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Head of Registry notes page 2.
facts.body: Head of Registry notes state 20 IVF clinics, of which six were public and 14 were private. The Q-IVF registry covers almost 100% of all Swedish IVF treatments performed at these 20 clinics. The registry has been collecting data since 2007 and the 2022 report is the 14th annual report. COVID-19 pandemic context: some clinics reduced capacity in 2020, contributing to the approximately 10% national volume decline.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Head of Registry notes, page 2 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: 20 IVF clinics, six were public, 14 were private.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the overall complication rate for IVF oocyte retrieval was 1.3% per OPU procedure, 145 complications out of 10,847 OPU. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 9 page 17.
facts.body: Figure 9 shows 1.3% of cycles reporting complications. Table 3 confirms: 145 complications reported out of 10,847 total OPU = 1.337%, which rounds to 1.3%. Specific complication types: OHSS requiring hospitalization = 37 cases at 0.34%; infections = 7 cases at 0.065%; bleeding = 10 cases at 0.092%. The report notes expected infection risk is 0.3% and expected major bleeding risk is 1-2 cases per 1,000 oocyte retrievals. COVID-19 context: 2020 volumes reduced approximately 10% nationally.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 9, page 17 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: 1.3% complication label. Cross-checked: 145/10,847=1.337%, rounds to 1.3%, delta acceptable.

facts.claim: In Sweden, the cumulative live birth rate per oocyte retrieval including all embryo transfers within one year after OPU for the 2019 OPU cohort was 43.2%. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 21a page 25.
facts.body: Figure 21a reports the cumulative LBR per oocyte retrieval for the most recent available cohort using 2019 OPU date = 43.2%. The 2020 treatment year data is not included in cumulative figures because the follow-up period has not elapsed. The denominator is not explicitly stated in the report. Figure 21b also reports live birth per first transfer, fresh or frozen within 6 months: 32.9% for the 2019 OPU cohort. The cumulative LBR series covers OPU dates 2007-2019.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 21a, page 25 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: most recent data point for 2019 OPU cohort cumulative LBR = 43.2%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 9,822 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles were started using own gametes. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Table 1 page 11.
facts.body: Table 1 breakdown: Standard IVF = 4,995 cycles; ICSI with ejaculated sperm = 4,541 cycles; ICSI with epididymal sperm = 286 cycles; total = 9,822 fresh own-gamete started cycles. 2020 data affected by COVID-19 pandemic which reduced volumes approximately 10% nationally. Cross-check verified: sum of components equals reported total.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2022-en.pdf. Component sum 4,995+4,541+286=9,822 matches reported total.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 6,890 frozen embryo transfer cycles were started using own gametes. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Table 1 page 11.
facts.body: Table 1 reports 6,890 started freeze cycle counts using own gametes in 2020. Combined with 9,822 fresh cycles, total own-gamete cycles = 16,712. Notably, for the first time in 2020, freeze cycles approached near-parity with fresh cycles, reflecting the growing trend toward freeze-all strategies. COVID-19 contributed to volume reduction from 2019 levels.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: Started cycles Freeze cycles own gametes = 6,890.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the live birth rate per insemination for IUI-D, all ages, was 15.5% out of 1,732 inseminations performed. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 28 page 33 and Table 8 page 32.
facts.body: Figure 28 reports the all-ages delivery rate per insemination for IUI-D = 15.5%. Denominator is inseminations performed at 1,732, not started cycles at 1,964. Cross-check: 268 deliveries / 1,732 inseminations = 15.47%, confirming the 15.5% figure with delta of 0.03 percentage points. Not all started cycles result in insemination; 232 cycle cancellations occurred. COVID-19 pandemic context: 2020 volumes reduced approximately 10% nationally.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 28, page 33 and Table 8, page 32 in qivf-2022-en.pdf. Cross-checked: 268/1,732=15.47%, matches 15.5% with delta 0.03pp.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 1,964 IUI-D intrauterine insemination with donor sperm cycles were started, resulting in 268 deliveries. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Table 8 page 32.
facts.body: Table 8 reports: IUI-D started cycles = 1,964; inseminations performed = 1,732; deliveries = 268. The live birth rate per insemination was 15.5%. Of the 1,964 started cycles, 756 were for single women. COVID-19 pandemic context: 2020 volumes were reduced approximately 10% nationally across all treatment types.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 8, page 32 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: IUI-D started cycles = 1,964; deliveries = 268.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the live birth rate per embryo transfer for fresh IVF/ICSI using own gametes, all ages, was 28.1% out of 6,415 embryo transfers. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 14 page 21.
facts.body: Figure 14 reports fresh IVF/ICSI all-ages live birth rate per embryo transfer = 28.1%. Denominator = 6,415 embryo transfers from Table 1. Cross-check: 1,795 deliveries / 6,415 ET = 27.98%, confirming 28.1% with minor rounding. Age-stratified rates: under 30 = 38.4%, 30-35 = 34.5%, 36-37 = 28.9%, 38-39 = 18.3%, 40-41 = 14.0%, 42+ = 7.8%. COVID-19 pandemic context: 2020 volumes reduced approximately 10% nationally.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 21 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: fresh IVF all ages = 28.1%. Cross-checked: 1,795/6,415=27.98%, delta 0.12pp acceptable rounding.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the live birth rate per embryo transfer for frozen-thawed IVF cycles using own gametes, all ages, was 36.1% out of 6,710 embryo transfers. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 14 page 21.
facts.body: Figure 14 reports freeze cycle all-ages live birth rate per embryo transfer = 36.1%. Denominator = 6,710 embryo transfers from Table 1. Cross-check: 2,417 deliveries / 6,710 ET = 36.02%, confirming 36.1% with minor rounding. Age-stratified rates: under 30 = 42.3%, 30-35 = 40.6%, 36-37 = 36.0%, 38-39 = 30.1%, 40-41 = 21.1%, 42+ = 17.5%. Frozen cycle LBR exceeded fresh cycle LBR by 8 percentage points, reflecting the benefit of optimal endometrial preparation and embryo selection in freeze-all protocols.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 14, page 21 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: freeze cycle all ages = 36.1%. Cross-checked: 2,417/6,710=36.02%, delta 0.08pp acceptable rounding.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the multiple birth rate for fresh IVF/ICSI cycles using own gametes was 2.82% per delivery. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 11a page 19.
facts.body: Figure 11a reports the fresh IVF/ICSI multiple birth rate = 2.82% twin rate. Despite an 89% SET rate in fresh cycles, some multiple births occur from double embryo transfers and monozygotic twinning from SET. Among fresh SET cycles specifically, the monozygotic twin rate was 1.15%. Sweden's low multiple birth rate compared to international averages reflects its long-standing commitment to elective single embryo transfer policy.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 11a, page 19 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: fresh IVF/ICSI twin rate = 2.82%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the OHSS hospitalization rate was 0.34% per oocyte retrieval: 37 cases requiring hospitalization out of 10,847 total OPU procedures. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, page 17 text and Table 3.
facts.body: Page 17 text states 37 cases of OHSS where hospitalization was required among fresh IVF treatments initiated in 2020. Denominator from Table 3: 10,847 total OPU procedures. Calculated rate: 37/10,847 = 0.34%. In context, 1,069 freeze-all cycles were performed in 2020 as a prophylactic strategy for patients at OHSS risk, up dramatically from 762 in 2019, suggesting that freeze-all protocols are actively reducing OHSS severity. The overall complication rate across all types was 1.3% per OPU.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 17 text, 37 OHSS hospitalization cases, and Table 3, 10,847 total OPU, in qivf-2022-en.pdf. Calculated: 37/10,847=0.34%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 859 oocyte freezing cycles were performed across all indications: 615 non-medical, 159 oncological, 56 other medical, 29 gender affirmation. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 30 page 35.
facts.body: Figure 30 provides the breakdown: non-medical indications = 615, confirmed by page 34 text stating 615 oocyte cryopreservations were performed for non-medical indications; oncological = 159; other medical = 56; gender change = 29; total = 859. Internal sum verified: 615+159+56+29=859. Social oocyte freezing constituted 71.6% of all oocyte freezing cycles. COVID-19 pandemic reduced overall treatment volumes but the trend toward social freezing continued to grow.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 30, page 35 in qivf-2022-en.pdf. Internal sum check: 615+159+56+29=859 confirmed.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 92% of IVF patients reported satisfaction with approach and treatment by staff, the highest-rated dimension in the Q-IVF patient satisfaction survey with n=1,969 respondents. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 37/41 pages 43-45.
facts.body: Figure 37/41 reports national aggregate satisfaction with approach and treatment by staff = 92% for the 2021 survey edition covering treatment year 2020. This was the highest-rated satisfaction dimension across all five categories measured. The survey had n=1,969 respondents, women only due to COVID-19 restrictions on partner clinic access. Other satisfaction dimensions: medical care = 90%, information = 85%, availability = 87%, participation in decision-making = 69% lowest. COVID-19 pandemic: partners excluded from clinic visits affected methodology.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 37/41, pages 43/45 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: approach/treatment satisfaction Totalt = 92%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 90% of IVF patients reported satisfaction with the medical care received, based on the Q-IVF patient satisfaction survey with n=1,969 respondents. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 37/38 pages 43-44.
facts.body: Figure 37/38 reports national aggregate satisfaction with medical care = 90% for the 2021 survey edition covering treatment year 2020. The survey had n=1,969 respondents, women only, as partners were largely excluded from clinic visits during COVID-19 pandemic, affecting methodology compared to prior years. Survey response rates: women 76%, partners 61%. Additional satisfaction dimensions: approach/treatment by staff = 92% highest, information = 85%, availability = 87%, participation in decision-making = 69% lowest.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 37/38, pages 43-44 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: medical care satisfaction Totalt = 90%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the single embryo transfer rate for fresh IVF/ICSI cycles using own gametes was 89%. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 10a page 18.
facts.body: Figure 10a reports the 2020 SET rate for fresh IVF cycles = 89%. This reflects Sweden's strong commitment to elective single embryo transfer policy to minimize multiple pregnancies. The corresponding fresh multiple birth rate was 2.82%, and among SET cycles specifically, the monozygotic twin rate was 1.15%. COVID-19 pandemic context: 2020 volumes reduced approximately 10% nationally but SET policy adherence remained high.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10a, page 18 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: 2020 SET rate fresh IVF = 89%.
