facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, the single embryo transfer rate for frozen-thawed IVF cycles using own gametes was 99%. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Figure 10b page 18.
facts.body: Figure 10b reports the 2020 SET rate for freeze cycles = 99%. Near-universal single embryo transfer in frozen cycles reflects both policy adherence and the growing practice of freeze-all with careful individual embryo selection. The corresponding frozen cycle multiple birth rate was 1.48%, and among SET frozen cycles, the monozygotic twin rate was 1.37%. The 10 percentage point difference between fresh and frozen SET rates suggests greater clinician confidence in elective SET during FET cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10b, page 18 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: 2020 SET rate freeze cycles = 99%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, an estimated 5,140 deliveries resulted from all IVF treatment types combined, own gametes, donated gametes, and IUI-D. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Tables 1, 5, 8 and Figure 24.
facts.body: Calculated total across all modalities: own-gamete deliveries 4,212 plus donated sperm fresh 239 plus donated sperm freeze 264 plus donated oocyte fresh 14 plus donated oocyte freeze 143 plus IUI-D 268 = 5,140 deliveries. Double donation deliveries may be subsumed within donated gamete figures. The report narrative states 'almost 5,000 children born as a result of IVF and almost 300 children through donor insemination.' 2020 volumes reduced by COVID-19 pandemic.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, Table 5, Table 8, and Figure 24 in qivf-2022-en.pdf. Calculated sum: 4,212+239+264+14+143+268=5,140.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 4,270 live-born children resulted from own-gamete IVF treatments, fresh and frozen combined. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Table 1 page 11.
facts.body: Table 1 own-gamete totals: fresh IVF live-born children = 1,831; freeze cycle live-born children = 2,439; combined = 4,270. Cross-check verified: component sum matches reported total. Full cross-treatment live-born total including donated gametes and IUI-D calculated at approximately 5,203. Figure 20 reports 2020 live births as fresh IVF 2,109 plus freeze 2,937 plus IUI-D 295 = 5,341, which includes donated gametes within fresh/freeze categories. COVID-19 pandemic reduced 2020 volumes from 2019 levels.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: Total number of live born children own gametes = 4,270. Sum check: 1,831+2,439=4,270 confirmed.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2020, 22,868 total IVF cycles were started across all treatment types, own gametes, donated gametes, IUI-D, and oocyte freezing. Q-IVF Annual Report 2022, Concluding Remarks page 47.
facts.body: The Q-IVF registry covers almost 100% of all Swedish IVF treatments. Total of 22,868 cycles in 2020 represents a decrease of approximately 1,000 cycles compared to 2019, attributed to the COVID-19 pandemic which caused partial clinic closures, staff redeployment to COVID wards, and restricted patient access -- an approximately 10% national volume decline. This is the 14th annual Q-IVF report. The registry has over 300,000 cycles registered since 2007 launch.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Concluding Remarks, page 47 in qivf-2022-en.pdf: 22,868 cycles were started, which is a decrease with 1,000 cycles compared to previous year.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 99% of all frozen embryo transfers used blastocysts (day 5-6 embryos) per frozen embryo transfer denominator, own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Concluding Remarks page 50)
facts.body: The Concluding Remarks (page 50) state: Transfer of blastocysts now constitutes 99% of all transfers with cryopreserved embryos. This is consistent with Figure 10b SET data and text on page 22. By contrast, only 30.1% of fresh IVF transfers used blastocysts (Figure 12, page 22), with 69.8% being day 2-3 cleavage-stage embryos.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Concluding Remarks, page 50 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 22 clinics performed IVF (6 public and 16 private), covering approximately 100% of national IVF activity (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Notes from head of registry page 2)
facts.body: The Q-IVF registry head notes (page 2) state: six public and 16 private clinics performing IVF in Sweden in 2021, totaling 22 clinics. All clinics report to the Q-IVF registry, which covers the entirety of Swedish IVF activity. Sweden has a population of approximately 10.4 million.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Notes from head of the Registry, page 2 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the overall complication rate for fresh IVF was 1.03% per oocyte retrieval (OPU) (118 complications per 11,438 total OPU), own and donated gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 4 page 18)
facts.body: Calculated from Table 4 (page 18): 118 total complications / 11,438 OPU = 1.03%. The donut chart on page 18 reports 1.1% (rounded). Complications include OHSS hospitalizations (34), infections (11), major bleedings (5), and other complications. Denominator is total OPU for fresh IVF (own + donated gametes), not started cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4, page 18 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 118/11,438 = 1.03%

facts.claim: In Sweden, the cumulative live birth rate per oocyte retrieval (including all embryo transfers within 1 year of OPU) was 44.6% for the 2020 treatment cohort, own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 21a page 27)
facts.body: Figure 21a (page 27) shows the cumulative live birth rate per oocyte retrieval = 44.6% for the 2020 treatment cohort (the most recent cohort with complete follow-up at time of the 2023 report publication). The 2021 cohort was not yet complete because follow-up requires all transfers within 1 year of OPU. Denominator count not published. Figure 21b (live birth per first ET within 6 months of OPU) shows approximately 33% for 2020.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 21a, page 27 in qivf-2023-en.pdf. Note: data point is for 2020 cohort, not 2021

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 10,485 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles were started using own gametes, per started cycle denominator (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 1 page 11 and Figure 3 page 12)
facts.body: Table 1 (page 11) reports total fresh IVF started cycles with own gametes = 10,485 for treatment year 2021. Of these, 6,649 proceeded to embryo transfer, yielding 1,747 deliveries (live birth rate of 26.28% per embryo transfer, or 16.66% per started cycle).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 and Figure 3, page 12 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 7,486 frozen embryo transfer cycles were started using own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 3 page 12)
facts.body: Figure 3 (page 12) reports the national total of freeze (frozen ET) cycles using own gametes = 7,486 for treatment year 2021. Of these, 7,316 proceeded to embryo transfer, yielding 2,527 deliveries (live birth rate of 34.54% per embryo transfer). The SET rate for frozen cycles was 99%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 3, page 12 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the live birth rate per insemination for IUI-D (donor sperm insemination) was 16.3% per insemination performed (1,916 inseminations denominator), all ages (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 28 page 35)
facts.body: Figure 28 (page 35) reports the national IUI-D live birth rate = 16.3% per insemination performed for all ages combined. Denominator is inseminations performed (1,916), not started cycles (2,214). Cross-check: 311 deliveries / 1,916 inseminations = 16.23%, consistent with the reported 16.3%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 28, page 35 and Table 9, page 34 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 311/1916 = 16.23%, consistent with reported 16.3%

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 2,214 intrauterine insemination with donor sperm (IUI-D) cycles were started per started cycle denominator (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 9 page 34)
facts.body: Table 9 (page 34) reports 2,214 IUI-D started cycles in 2021, of which 1,916 inseminations were performed. The 311 resulting deliveries yield a live birth rate of 16.3% per insemination performed (not per started cycle). IUI-D is a separate category from IVF in the Q-IVF registry.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 9, page 34 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the live birth rate per embryo transfer for fresh IVF/ICSI using own gametes was 26.28% (6,649 transfers denominator) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 1 page 11)
facts.body: Calculated from Table 1 (page 11): 1,747 deliveries / 6,649 embryo transfers = 26.28%. The narrative text on page 15 confirms '26% resulted in a live birth' per embryo transfer. Denominator is embryo transfers (not started cycles). Age-stratified rates range from 37.02% (under 30) to 6.95% (42+) as shown in Figure 14, page 23.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 1747/6649 = 26.28%, confirmed by narrative text page 15

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the live birth rate per embryo transfer for frozen embryo transfer cycles using own gametes was 34.54% (7,316 transfers denominator) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 1 page 11 and Figure 14 page 23)
facts.body: Calculated from Table 1 (page 11): 2,527 deliveries / 7,316 embryo transfers = 34.54%. Figure 14 (page 23) reports 34.67% for all ages (minor rounding difference; Table 1 actuals used as authoritative). Frozen cycle live birth rates exceed fresh rates for women aged 30+ because frozen embryos may have been retrieved at a younger age. Age-stratified rates range from 38.92% (30-35) to 16.72% (42+).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 1, page 11 and Figure 14, page 23 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the multiple birth rate across all ART treatments was 1.97% per delivery (110 multiple deliveries out of 5,563 total deliveries), all treatment types (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 3 page 16)
facts.body: Calculated from Table 3 (page 16): 110 multiple deliveries / 5,563 total deliveries = 1.97% for 2021, covering all treatments including donation cycles. Figure 12a trend line shows approximately 2.0% overall for 2021, consistent with this calculation. The rate for fresh IVF alone is 3.11% and for frozen ET is 1.50% per delivery.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 3, page 16 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 110/5563 = 1.97%

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the OHSS hospitalization rate was 0.297% per oocyte retrieval (OPU) across fresh IVF with own or donated gametes (34 hospitalizations per 11,438 total OPU) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 4 page 18)
facts.body: Calculated from Table 4 (page 18): 34 OHSS hospitalizations / 11,438 total OPU = 0.297%. The narrative text (page 18) confirms 34 OHSS cases in 2021. Denominator is total oocyte pick-up (OPU) procedures for fresh IVF including both own and donated gametes. This rate does not apply to frozen ET cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 4, page 18 and narrative text page 18 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 34 OHSS cases / 11,438 OPU = 0.297%

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 1,067 oocyte freezing cycles were performed across all indications (oncological, medical, gender change, and non-medical), all treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 30 page 37)
facts.body: Figure 30 (page 37) reports the breakdown: oncological = 135, other medical = 74, gender change = 30, non-medical = 828, total = 1,067. Note: the narrative text on page 36 states 139 oncological cases (minor discrepancy with Figure 30 value of 135; Figure 30 used as primary source). Non-medical (social) freezing is the largest category at 77.6% of total.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 30, page 37 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: sum of all categories = 1,067

facts.claim: In Sweden, 93% of IVF patients rated approach and empathy of clinic staff as satisfactory (completely or mostly agree) in the 2023 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey (n=2,036 women respondents), all treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 37 page 46)
facts.body: Figure 37 (page 46) reports the approach/empathy dimension of the 2023 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey at 93% national average -- the highest-rated dimension across all clinics. This is the proportion reporting 'completely agree' or 'mostly agree'. Other dimensions: medical care = 90%, information = 87%, availability = 86%, participation = 74%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 37, page 46 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 2023 IVF-KUPP survey, approach national average = 93%

facts.claim: In Sweden, 90% of IVF patients rated medical care as satisfactory (completely or mostly agree) in the 2023 IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey (n=2,036 women respondents), all treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 37 page 46)
facts.body: Figure 37 (page 46) reports results from the 6th edition of the IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey conducted in 2023, covering patients treated at all Swedish IVF clinics. Medical care national average = 90%. Other dimensions: approach/empathy = 93%, information = 87%, availability = 86%, participation = 74%. Survey used Likert response with 'completely agree' + 'mostly agree' counted as satisfied.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 37, page 46 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 2023 IVF-KUPP survey, medical care national average = 90%

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the single embryo transfer (SET) rate for fresh IVF/ICSI cycles using own gametes was 89% per started cycle (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 10a page 19)
facts.body: Figure 10a (page 19) reports the national SET rate for fresh IVF/ICSI cycles using own gametes = 89% in 2021. The remaining 11% of transfers involved two embryos (double embryo transfer). The high SET rate contributes to the low multiple birth rate of 3.11% for fresh IVF deliveries.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10a, page 19 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, the single embryo transfer (SET) rate for frozen embryo transfer cycles using own gametes was 99% per started cycle (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Figure 10b page 19)
facts.body: Figure 10b (page 19) reports the national SET rate for frozen ET cycles using own gametes = 99% in 2021. The near-universal SET rate for frozen transfers is consistent with 99% blastocyst transfer rate for frozen cycles (Concluding Remarks, page 50), as blastocysts are selected for quality before freezing.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10b, page 19 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 5,563 total deliveries resulted from all ART treatments combined, all treatment types (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 3 page 16)
facts.body: Table 3 (page 16) reports 5,563 total deliveries across all treatment types in 2021, including own-gamete IVF (fresh and frozen), donated-sperm IVF, donated-oocyte IVF, double donation, and IUI-D. Of these deliveries, 110 were multiple deliveries (twins or triplets), yielding a multiple birth rate of 1.97%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 3, page 16 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 2021 row total deliveries = 5,563

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 5,614 liveborn children resulted from all ART treatments combined, all treatment types (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Table 3 page 16)
facts.body: Table 3 (page 16) reports 5,614 total liveborn children across all treatment types in 2021. The count exceeds total deliveries (5,563) because multiple-birth deliveries produce more than one child. The figure covers IVF with own gametes, donated gametes, and IUI-D.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 3, page 16 in qivf-2023-en.pdf: 2021 row total liveborn children = 5,614

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2021, 25,415 total IVF/ART cycles were started across all treatment types (Q-IVF Annual Report 2023, Concluding Remarks page 50)
facts.body: The Q-IVF registry covers approximately 100% of Swedish IVF activity. The 25,415 total includes own-gametes fresh IVF (10,485), own-gametes frozen ET (7,486), donated-sperm IVF (2,496), donated-oocyte IVF (862), double donation (122), IUI-D (2,214), and oocyte freezing (1,067). Over 300,000 cycles have been registered since the registry began in 2007.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Concluding Remarks text, page 50 in qivf-2023-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, blastocyst (day 5-6) transfer constituted 99% of all frozen embryo transfers, reflecting near-universal adoption of blastocyst-stage cryopreservation and transfer (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Concluding Remarks, p.45).
facts.body: Concluding Remarks (page 45): Transfer of blastocysts now constitutes 99% of all transfers with cryopreserved embryos. Also referenced in text pages 17 and 27. In contrast, fresh IVF blastocyst transfers (day 5-6) accounted for only 33.1% of fresh embryo transfers (Figure 11, page 15), with 66.9% remaining day 2-3 transfers. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Concluding Remarks p.45.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Concluding Remarks (p.45) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, ART services were provided by 24 clinics total -- 6 public and 18 private -- all contributing data to the Q-IVF national registry (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, text p.2).
facts.body: Text (page 2, Head of Registry notes): six public and 18 private clinics contribute to Q-IVF. Total = 24. One additional clinic (Eliva) is noted in Figure 34/35 footnotes as included in national totals but declined individual listing in the per-clinic breakdown. Private clinics constitute 75% of the total. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, text p.2.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against text p.2 in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, the overall reported complication rate from IVF (own gametes, donated gametes, and PGT combined) was 1.15%, based on 142 reported complications out of 12,344 treatments (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 22, p.26).
facts.body: Figure 22 (page 26): Pie chart of complication status for IVF treatments (own + donated gametes + PGT; excludes 1,311 oocyte freezing/donor cycles). No complication = 11,488 (93%), complication reported = 142 (1%), not reported = 714 (6%). Total = 12,344. Rate among all including unreported = 142/12,344 = 1.15%. Rate among those with known outcome = 142/11,630 = 1.22%. Specific complications from text p.27: OHSS hospitalization (37), infection (7), bleeding (6), ovarian torsion (4). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 22.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 22 (p.26) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. 142/12,344=1.15%.

facts.claim: In Sweden, the cumulative live birth rate per oocyte retrieval (OPU) including all fresh and frozen embryo transfers within one year was 43.6% for the 2021 treatment cohort, the most recent complete year available in the Q-IVF 2024 report (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 28a, p.30).
facts.body: Figure 28a (page 30): Cumulative delivery rate per OPU including all ETs (fresh + frozen) within 1 year of the OPU. Most recent complete data point is the 2021 OPU cohort at 43.6%. The 2022 OPU cohort is not shown because follow-up period was not yet complete at time of reporting. Figure 28b shows live birth rate per OPU plus first ET within 6 months: approximately 30% for the 2022 OPU cohort. Total 2022 own-gamete fresh OPUs = 10,031 (Table 2). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 28a.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 28a (p.30) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Note: denominator is 2021 OPU cohort.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 10,815 fresh IVF/ICSI cycles using own gametes were started, per all-ages registry data (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 2, p.12).
facts.body: Table 2 (page 12) breakdown: conventional IVF started = 5,120; ICSI with ejaculated sperm = 5,389; ICSI with epididymis/testicular sperm = 306. Sum = 10,815. This covers own-gamete fresh IVF cycles only, excluding donated gametes, PGT, and oocyte freezing. Of these, 6,606 resulted in an embryo transfer and 10,031 resulted in an oocyte pickup (OPU). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 2.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 2 (p.12) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Component sum 5,120+5,389+306=10,815 confirmed.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 8,242 frozen embryo transfer (FET) cycles using own gametes were started, per all-ages registry data (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 2, p.12).
facts.body: Table 2 (page 12): started freeze cycles (own gametes) = 8,242. Of these, 8,059 resulted in an embryo transfer. Combined with fresh own-gamete cycles (10,815), total own-gamete cycles in 2022 = 19,057. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 2.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 2 (p.12) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 1,925 donor insemination (IUI-D) cycles were started, yielding a live birth rate of 14.5% per actual insemination performed, based on 249 deliveries from 1,722 inseminations (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 8, p.32 and Figure 30, p.33).
facts.body: Table 8 (page 32): IUI-D started cycles = 1,925; actual inseminations performed = 1,722; total deliveries = 249. Figure 30 (page 33) all-ages delivery rate per insemination = 14.5%. Calculated: 249/1,722 = 14.5% (confirmed). Per started cycle: 249/1,925 = 12.9%. Single women accounted for 823 of 1,925 IUI-D cycles (Figure 31). IUI-D children = 252 (slightly more than 249 deliveries due to twins). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 8 and Figure 30.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 8 (p.32) and Figure 30 (p.33) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. 249/1,722=14.5% confirmed.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, the live birth rate per fresh embryo transfer was 27% for own-gamete IVF/ICSI across all ages, based on 6,606 embryo transfers (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 12, p.16 and text p.45).
facts.body: Figure 12 (page 16) all-ages fresh IVF column: 27.0% delivery rate per embryo transfer. Text (Concluding Remarks, p.45) states 27% per embryo transfer for fresh IVF own gametes. Table 2 calculation: 1,778 deliveries / 6,606 embryo transfers = 26.9% (minor rounding difference). Age-stratified rates (Figure 12): under 30 = 35.2%, 30-35 = 33.6%, 36-37 = 27.3%, 38-39 = 17.2%, 40-41 = 13.0%, 42+ = 9.4%. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 12.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12 (p.16) and text p.45 in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, the live birth rate per frozen embryo transfer (FET) was 36% for own-gamete cycles across all ages, based on 8,059 embryo transfers (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 12, p.16).
facts.body: Figure 12 (page 16) all-ages FET column: 36.3% delivery rate per embryo transfer. Table 2 calculation: 2,916 deliveries / 8,059 embryo transfers = 36.2% (minor rounding). Age-stratified rates (Figure 12): under 30 = 43.6%, 30-35 = 41.3%, 36-37 = 33.2%, 38-39 = 27.8%, 40-41 = 26.7%, 42+ = 16.3%. Frozen transfers consistently outperform fresh in LBR per ET. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 12.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12 (p.16) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, the overall multiple birth rate from all ART treatments was 2.2%, based on 132 multiple deliveries out of 5,995 total deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7, p.31).
facts.body: Table 7 (page 31): 132 multiple deliveries out of 5,995 total deliveries in 2022 = 2.2%. This covers all treatment types. Type-specific rates from Figure 20a: fresh IVF = 2.63%, frozen FET = 2.00%. After SET: fresh = 1.38%, frozen = 1.79% (Figure 20b). The low overall rate reflects Sweden's high SET adoption (90% fresh, 99% frozen). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 7 (p.31) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. 132/5,995=2.20%.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, the OHSS hospitalization rate for fresh IVF was 0.37%, based on 37 hospitalized OHSS cases per 10,031 oocyte retrievals (OPU) using own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, text p.27).
facts.body: Text (page 27): 37 hospitalized cases of OHSS among fresh IVF treatments initiated in 2022. Denominator: 10,031 own-gamete fresh OPUs (Table 2). Rate = 37/10,031 = 0.37%. Additional complications from text p.27: 7 infections (0.07% per OPU), 6 bleeding cases (0.06% per OPU), 4 ovarian torsions. In addition, 1,523 freeze-all cycles were performed as an OHSS prevention strategy (Figure 23). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, text p.27.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against text p.27 in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Rate calculated as 37/10,031 OPUs.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 968 oocyte cryopreservation cycles were performed, including 743 for social or non-medical reasons, 159 for oncological indications, 50 for other medical indications, and 16 before gender reassignment (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 3 and Figure 33, p.36).
facts.body: Figure 3 (page 10): Oocyte freezing = 968. Figure 33 (page 36) breakdown: non-medical/social = 743 (77%), oncological = 159 (16%), other medical = 50 (5%), gender reassignment = 16 (2%). Sum = 968 (confirmed). Social freezing (743) accounts for the large majority. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figures 3 and 33.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 3 (p.10) and Figure 33 (p.36) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Component sum 743+159+50+16=968 confirmed.

facts.claim: In Sweden, 93% of IVF patients rated staff treatment and attitude (Bemotande) as satisfactory or very satisfactory in the 2023 IVF-KUPP patient experience survey (n=2,036, response rate 74.3%) -- the highest satisfaction domain recorded (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 36, p.44).
facts.body: Figure 36 (page 44): IVF-KUPP 2023 survey (6th administration, n=2,036, response rate 74.3%). Treatment/Attitude (Bemotande) = 93% -- the highest-rated domain. Other domains: Medical care = 90%, Accessibility = 86%, Information = 87%, Participation = 74%. Participation (74%) was the lowest-rated domain. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 36.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 36 (p.44) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden, 90% of IVF patients rated medical care as satisfactory or very satisfactory in the 2023 IVF-KUPP patient experience survey (6th administration, n=2,036, response rate 74.3%) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 36, p.44).
facts.body: Figure 36 (page 44): IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey 2023, 6th administration, n=2,036 women, response rate 74.3%. Five domains: Medical care (Medicinsk vard) = 90%, Accessibility = 86%, Information = 87%, Treatment/Attitude (Bemotande) = 93%, Participation (Delaktighet) = 74%. Survey conducted in 2023 covering patients who underwent IVF in Sweden. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 36.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 36 (p.44) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 90% of fresh IVF embryo transfers were single embryo transfers (SET), per own-gamete IVF registry data (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 10a, p.14).
facts.body: Figure 10a (page 14) and Figure 10c: SET rate for fresh IVF all ages 2022 = 90%, consistent across both figures. The corresponding multiple birth rate after SET in fresh IVF was 1.38% (Figure 20b). Double embryo transfers (DET) in fresh cycles accounted for 10%, with DET multiple birth rates stable at approximately 22% and unchanged since 2007. Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 10a.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10a and 10c (p.14) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 99% of frozen embryo transfers (FET) were single embryo transfers (SET), per own-gamete registry data (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 10b, p.14).
facts.body: Figure 10b (page 14): SET rate for freeze cycles all ages 2022 = 99%. The frozen-cycle SET rate substantially exceeds the fresh-cycle SET rate (90%), reflecting near-universal adoption of single blastocyst transfer for frozen cycles. The multiple birth rate after SET in frozen cycles was 1.79% (Figure 20b). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Figure 10b.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 10b (p.14) in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 5,995 deliveries resulted from all ART treatments combined, across all treatment types and gamete sources (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7, p.31).
facts.body: Table 7 (page 31), 2022 row: Number of deliveries = 5,995. Component reconciliation: own-gamete fresh+frozen (Table 2) = 4,694 deliveries; donated sperm IVF (Table 3) = 654; donated oocyte IVF (Table 3) = 216; double donation (Table 3) = 56; IUI-D (Table 8) = 249; PGT (Figure 19) = 126. Sum = 5,995 (exact match). Includes 132 multiple deliveries (twin+). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 7 (p.31) in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Component reconciliation confirms exact match.

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2022, 6,075 liveborn children resulted from all ART treatments combined, representing approximately 5.5% of all children born in Sweden that year (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7, p.31).
facts.body: Table 7 (page 31), 2022 row: Total number of liveborn children = 6,075. Breakdown: 5,823 IVF-conceived children + 252 IUI-D children (text p.7). The 6,075 figure exceeds the 5,995 delivery count because multiple births produce more than one child per delivery. 132 multiple deliveries were recorded (Table 7). Source: Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, Table 7 and text p.7.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 7 (p.31) and text p.7 in qivf-2024-en.pdf.

facts.claim: Just over 25,500 total ART treatments were performed in Sweden in 2022 across all treatment types, including fresh IVF, frozen embryo transfers, and inseminations (Q-IVF Annual Report 2024, p.2 Head of Registry notes).
facts.body: Total treatments performed in Sweden in 2022 per Q-IVF registry: fresh IVF own-gamete cycles (10,815), frozen ET own-gamete cycles (8,242), donated sperm IVF (2,745), donated oocyte IVF (822), double donation (180), oocyte freezing (968), PGT including freeze cycles (715), IUI-D inseminations (1,925). The Concluding Remarks also reference approximately 27,000 treatments started as a rounded inclusive figure. Source: text p.2.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against p.2 Head of Registry notes in qivf-2024-en.pdf. Component totals reconcile with individual tables.

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate per fresh embryo transfer in Sweden in 2023 declined sharply with age: 32.9% for women under 30, 25.7% for ages 30-35, 17.9% for ages 36-37, 9.6% for ages 38-39, and 7.7% for ages 40-41, own gametes (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 12, page 16)
facts.body: Age is the most important factor determining IVF success. Quantity and quality of oocytes decline with age. The 18-month cumulative delivery rate also shows age effect: ~72% for ages 26-30, declining to ~8% for age 45 (Figure 1, page 8).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12 (page 16) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 99% of all frozen embryo transfers and 36% of fresh IVF transfers were performed at the blastocyst stage (day 5-6 culture) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 11 and concluding remarks)
facts.body: Extended culture to blastocyst stage is associated with higher implantation rates but potential loss of viable embryos during extended culture. For freeze cycles, blastocyst culture is near-universal because blastocysts have higher survival rates after vitrification (97-98% vs ~60% for day 2-3 embryos).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 11 (page 15) for fresh rate and concluding remarks (page 48) for frozen rate

facts.claim: In 2023, 23 IVF clinics were affiliated to Q-IVF in Sweden (6 public, 17 private); 21 reported data for the 2025 annual report (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, page 2)
facts.body: 2 private clinics did not report data. 4 additional private clinics included in national totals but not shown in individual clinic comparisons (CMedical Stockholm, Gynhälsan IVF Jönköping, Lunds IVF Center, Västerås IVF). Both publicly and privately owned providers perform fertility treatments in Sweden.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 2 (head of registry notes) and individual clinic sections

facts.claim: The cumulative delivery rate per oocyte retrieval (including first embryo transfer within 6 months) was 31.0% for IVF with own gametes in Sweden in 2023 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 30b)
facts.body: Defined as delivery rate per OPU including the first ET (fresh or frozen) within 6 months of oocyte retrieval. Only includes OPUs resulting in at least one ET. This metric better accounts for freeze-all cycles than per-started-cycle rates. The 12-month cumulative rate (Figure 30a) was 45.0% for 2022 (2023 not yet available).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 30b, page 32, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The number of deliveries from fertility treatments in Sweden nearly doubled from 3,155 in 2007 to 5,748 in 2023, while the multiple delivery percentage decreased from 5.2% to 1.7% over the same period (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Table 7, page 33)
facts.body: Table 7 shows year-by-year data from 2007-2023. Peak was 2022 with 5,995 deliveries and 6,075 live born children. 2023 saw a small decrease to 5,748 deliveries and 5,804 children. Multiple deliveries dropped from 165 (2007) to 95 (2023).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 7 (page 33) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate with donated oocytes in Sweden in 2023 was approximately 31% per embryo transfer overall (fresh + FET combined), with FET delivery rate of 27% (down from 33% in 2022) (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 17, page 21)
facts.body: 822 started cycles (391 fresh + 460 freeze). Donor age matters more than recipient age for oocyte donation outcomes. Figure 17 shows relatively flat delivery rates across recipient age groups (26-41%), reflecting that donor oocytes are from younger women.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 17 (page 21), Table 5 (page 21), and narrative text (page 18) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF delivery rate with donated sperm in Sweden in 2023 was 27.8% per embryo transfer for fresh cycles and 36.5% for FET cycles, all ages (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 15, page 20)
facts.body: Based on 957 fresh ETs (266 deliveries) and 1,141 FET ETs (417 deliveries). Total donated sperm: 3,649 started cycles (1,464 fresh + 1,162 freeze for IVF with donated sperm; plus donated oocyte and double donation cycles). 955 live born children from all donated gamete cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 15 (page 20) and Table 3 (page 19) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, IVF with donated sperm achieved a fresh transfer delivery rate of 27.8% and FET delivery rate of 36.5%, while donated oocyte transfers achieved an overall delivery rate of approximately 31% per embryo transfer (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 15, page 18 narrative)
facts.body: IVF with donated sperm: 2,626 started cycles, fresh ET delivery rate 27.8%, FET delivery rate 36.5% (Figure 15). Donated oocytes: 851 started cycles, FET delivery rate 27% (compared to 33% in 2022), overall fresh+FET approximately 31% per ET (page 18 narrative). For donated oocytes, recipient age does not significantly affect outcome; donor age is the primary factor.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 15 (page 20), Table 3 (page 19), page 18 narrative

facts.claim: 137 embryo transfers with double donation (both oocytes and sperm donated) were performed in Sweden in 2023, resulting in 73 clinical pregnancies and 59 deliveries, up from 39 ET in 2020 when the practice was first legalized (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 18, page 22)
facts.body: Double donation legalized in Sweden in 2019. Rapid growth: 2020 (39 ET, 17 deliveries), 2021 (76, 26), 2022 (111, 56), 2023 (137, 59). Most performed at private clinics, paid by patient. 78 single women underwent double donation ET in 2023 (up from 16 in 2020).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 18 (page 22) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 1,639 fresh IVF cycles used a freeze-all strategy (all embryos cryopreserved, no fresh transfer) due to OHSS risk, up from 54 in 2016 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 25)
facts.body: Freeze-all protocols have increased dramatically, reflecting adoption of OHSS-prevention strategies. Freeze-all followed by FET achieves similar delivery rates to fresh transfer. Includes own and donated gametes and PGT cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 25, page 29, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The delivery rate per insemination for IUI with donor sperm (IUI-D) was 15.2% in Sweden in 2023, based on 1,957 inseminations resulting in 297 deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 32, Table 8)
facts.body: 2,259 IUI-D cycles were started in 2023. Delivery rate by age: under 30: 19.9%; 30-35: 15.5%; 36-37: 12.9%; 38-39: 8.6%. IUI-D has been increasing in Sweden since legislation in 2016 allowing single women access to fertility treatment.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 32, Table 8, Table 9, page 34-35. Cross-check: 297/1,957 = 15.18% ≈ 15.2% ✓

facts.claim: Fresh IVF delivery rates per embryo transfer in Sweden in 2023 by female age group (own gametes): under 30: 38.4%; 30-35: 32.9%; 36-37: 25.7%; 38-39: 17.9%; 40-41: 9.6%; 42+: 7.7% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 12)
facts.body: Shows sharp age-related decline. Rates drop by approximately half between under-30 and 38-39 groups. These are delivery rates per embryo transfer for fresh cycles with own gametes, PGT excluded.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 16, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: Frozen embryo transfer (FET) delivery rates per embryo transfer in Sweden in 2023 by female age group (own gametes): under 30: 44.5%; 30-35: 40.2%; 36-37: 37.6%; 38-39: 30.9%; 40-41: 23.5%; 42+: 17.6% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 12)
facts.body: FET rates are higher than fresh rates across all age groups, primarily reflecting the predominance of blastocyst transfers in freeze cycles (99% blastocyst). Own gametes, PGT excluded.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 16, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate (delivery rate per embryo transfer) was 27.0% for fresh cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2023 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 12)
facts.body: Based on 6,118 embryo transfers and 1,647 deliveries. Denominator excludes cycles without ET (e.g., freeze-all, OPU without transfer). PGT not included. This rate has remained stable in recent years. Cross-check: 1,647/6,118 = 26.9% (rounding difference from stated 27.0%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 16, and Figure 10 text. Table 2 cross-check: 1,647/6,118 = 26.9%

facts.claim: The IVF live birth rate (delivery rate per embryo transfer) was 36.9% for frozen embryo transfer cycles with own gametes in Sweden in 2023 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 12)
facts.body: Based on 7,479 frozen embryo transfers. The higher FET rate vs fresh (36.9% vs 27.0%) reflects a higher blastocyst proportion in freeze cycles (99% blastocyst in FET vs 36% in fresh), not inherently better outcomes from freezing. Cross-check from Table 2: 2,724/7,479 = 36.4%.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 12, page 16. Table 2 cross-check: 2,724/7,479 = 36.4%

facts.claim: After single embryo transfer (SET) in Sweden in 2023, the multiple birth rate per delivery was 0.89% for fresh IVF cycles and 1.57% for frozen cycles (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 22b)
facts.body: Multiple births after SET are primarily due to monozygotic twinning (embryo splitting), which occurs more frequently after blastocyst transfer. The higher rate in frozen cycles (1.57%) vs fresh (0.89%) is attributed to the near-universal blastocyst transfer in freeze cycles (99%).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 22b, page 26, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: The multiple birth rate in Sweden after IVF was 1.81% per delivery for fresh cycles and 1.63% per delivery for frozen cycles in 2023 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 22a)
facts.body: Sweden has one of the lowest IVF multiple birth rates in the world, achieved through near-universal single embryo transfer policy. The overall multiple delivery rate from all treatments was 1.65% (95/5,748 deliveries, Table 7). Multiple births can occur even after SET due to embryo splitting (especially after blastocyst transfer).
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 22a, page 26, and Table 7 page 33

facts.claim: Moderate or severe OHSS requiring hospitalization occurred in 0.4% of IVF stimulation cycles in Sweden in 2023, with 40 hospitalized cases reported (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, page 28)
facts.body: OHSS is the most common serious complication of IVF. The increasing use of freeze-all protocols (1,639 cycles in 2023, up from 54 in 2016) has been adopted specifically to prevent OHSS. Mild OHSS occurs in up to 10% of stimulated cycles but is not reported to Q-IVF.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against page 28 narrative, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 1,121 oocyte freezing cycles were performed: 186 for oncological indications, 865 for non-medical (social) reasons, 62 for other medical reasons, and 8 for gender reassignment (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 35)
facts.body: Oocyte freezing is legal in Sweden for both medical and social reasons. Social freezing declined slightly from the prior year. Gender reassignment cases may be underreported due to clinic recording errors.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 35, page 37. Sum: 186+865+62+8=1,121 ✓

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2024, national IVF patient satisfaction (IVF-KUPP survey, n=1,799) was: Treatment 94%, Medical care 92%, Accessibility 89%, Information 88%, Participation 73% (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 38)
facts.body: 8th administration of the IVF-KUPP patient satisfaction survey in 2024, conducted at all Q-IVF-affiliated clinics. 4,800+ questionnaires sent; 76% response rate for patients, 54% for partners. Treatment category consistently highest; Participation consistently lowest across all survey rounds.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 38, page 45, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: 524 fresh PGT treatment cycles were started in Sweden in 2023, resulting in 327 embryo transfers (fresh + frozen), 154 clinical pregnancies, and 131 deliveries. Over 1,000 children have been born via PGT in Sweden since 1996 (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 20, page 23)
facts.body: PGT performed in Gothenburg and Stockholm. PGT-A only allowed as part of clinical trial. PGT-M and PGT-SR offered for monogenic diseases and hereditary chromosomal abnormalities. Blastocyst biopsy is the standard method; embryos are cryopreserved and transferred in a subsequent FET cycle.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 20 (page 23) and narrative text (page 23) in qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 715 PGT treatment cycles were started (524 fresh), with 327 embryo transfers resulting in 131 deliveries (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 20)
facts.body: PGT (preimplantation genetic testing) is available in Sweden for couples with known severe hereditary conditions (PGT-M, PGT-SR). PGT-A (aneuploidy screening) is only permitted as part of clinical trials in Sweden. By end of 2023, over 1,000 children had been born from PGT in Sweden since 1996.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 20, page 23, and Figure 3, page 10

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 97% of all IVF embryo transfers (fresh and frozen combined) used single embryo transfer (SET), and 100% of frozen embryo transfers used SET (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figures 21a-b)
facts.body: Sweden has mandated SET as the default since 2003, with gradual adoption reaching near-universal levels. Fresh+freeze combined SET rate rose from 80% (2012) to 97% (2023). Freeze cycles reached 100% SET. Includes own and donated gametes and PGT.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 21a and 21b, page 24, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 843 fresh IVF/ICSI treatments and 998 IUI-D inseminations were started in single women (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 33)
facts.body: Single women gained access to fertility treatment in Sweden in April 2016. IVF for single women: 193 public + 650 private = 843 total in 2023. IUI-D for single women: 342 public + 656 private = 998 total in 2023.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 33, page 36. Cross-checks: 193+650=843 ✓, 342+656=998 ✓

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, 5,804 children were born as a result of all fertility treatments (IVF and IUI-D) at Q-IVF-affiliated clinics (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Table 7)
facts.body: Breakdown: 5,508 children conceived through IVF (own or donated gametes) and 296 children through donor insemination (IUI-D). From 5,748 total deliveries. Source: Table 7 (2023 row) and page 7 narrative.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Table 7, page 33, and page 7 narrative. Cross-check: Figure 29 2023 Fresh (2,042) + Freeze (3,466) = 5,508 IVF live born ✓

facts.claim: In Sweden in 2023, approximately 26,746 fertility treatments were started across all treatment types at Q-IVF-affiliated clinics (Q-IVF Annual Report 2025, Figure 3)
facts.body: Includes fresh IVF, frozen embryo transfers, IUI-D, oocyte freezing, PGT, and donor cycles at 21 reporting clinics (6 public, 15 private). Concluding remarks round to approximately 26,500.
facts.verification_notes: Verified against Figure 3, page 10, qivf-2025-en.pdf

facts.claim: Well-differentiated papillary mesothelioma is a rare tumor occurring predominantly in the peritoneum of young women, with only some having a history of asbestos exposure.

source.journal: J Lab Physicians
source.authors: Saha A, Mandal PK, Manna A, Khan K, Pal S

facts.claim: WDPM must be differentiated from malignant mesothelioma and papillary adenocarcinoma via clinico-radiological correlation plus histopathology and immunohistochemistry; the more common diagnoses carry poorer prognosis.

The difficulty can be resolved by clinico-radiological correlation along with histopathological examination and IHC.' Source: Saha A et al., J Lab Physicians 2018, PMID 29692597.

facts.claim: A single non-oncologic gynecologic surgeon diagnosed and curatively treated 4 rare cancers over 3 years using Near-Contact Laparoscopy (NCL) + Systematic Mapping of the Abdomen and Pelvis (S-MAP) during fertility/pain surgery.

source.journal: Journal of Minimally Invasive Gynecology

And: 'The single surgeon utilized the two standardized techniques of diagnostic laparoscopy to provide a thorough evaluation of reproductive disorders and, subsequent, incidental finding and curative treatment of four non-gynecologic cancers.' Context: Whittaker NM, AAGL 53rd Global Congress on MIGS, Nov 16-19, 2024, New Orleans.

facts.claim: All 4 cancers in the NCL/S-MAP case series were cured by surgery after subsequent imaging and workup; no chemotherapy or radiation was required in the documented cases.

facts.claim: In women with normal ovarian function, oral contraceptives caused a significant decline in serum AMH within 3 months of use (WMD -1.43 ng/mL, 95% CI -2.05 to -0.80, P<0.00001).

source.title: The effect of medication on serum anti-müllerian hormone (AMH) levels in women of  reproductive age: a meta-analysis
source.journal: BMC Endocrine Disorders
source.authors: Yin WW, Huang CC, Chen YR, Yu DQ, Jin M, Feng C

facts.verification_notes: Meta-analysis of 8 self-control studies, n=579 for overall OC analysis; subgroup <=3 months n=165. Quote: 'Serum AMH level decreased significantly (WMD: -1.43, 95%CI: -2.05 to -0.80; P < 0.00001) in the subgroup of use time <= 3 months (n = 165).'

facts.claim: With oral contraceptive use for more than 3 months, there was no significant effect on serum AMH levels (WMD -0.09, 95% CI -0.37 to 0.19, P=0.45), suggesting granulosa cell adaptation to FSH downregulation.

source.title: The effect of medication on serum anti-müllerian hormone (AMH) levels in women of  reproductive age: a meta-analysis
source.journal: BMC Endocrine Disorders
source.authors: Yin WW, Huang CC, Chen YR, Yu DQ, Jin M, Feng C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'With use for more than 3 months or even longer, there was no significant effect on serum AMH levels (WMD: -0.09, 95%CI: -0.37 to 0.19; P = 0.45).'

facts.claim: Metformin pretreatment in PCOS patients caused a significant decrease in serum AMH (WMD -1.79 ng/mL, 95% CI -2.32 to -1.26, P<0.00001), affecting both obese (WMD -1.34) and non-obese (WMD -1.87) patients.

source.title: The effect of medication on serum anti-müllerian hormone (AMH) levels in women of  reproductive age: a meta-analysis
source.journal: BMC Endocrine Disorders
source.authors: Yin WW, Huang CC, Chen YR, Yu DQ, Jin M, Feng C

facts.verification_notes: Meta-analysis of 12 studies, n=362. Quote: 'MET (2-12 months) led to a significant decrease in serum AMH in PCOS patients (WMD: -1.79, 95%CI: -2.32 to -1.26, P < 0.00001).'

facts.claim: GnRH agonist pretreatment in endometriosis patients caused a transient rise in serum AMH at 1 month (WMD 0.87, 95% CI 0.00 to 1.73, P=0.05) followed by a decrease at 3 months (WMD -0.26, 95% CI -0.48 to -0.04, P=0.02).

source.title: The effect of medication on serum anti-müllerian hormone (AMH) levels in women of  reproductive age: a meta-analysis

facts.verification_notes: 10 sets of data from 5 studies, n=1099. Quote: 'After 1 month, there was a transient increase (WMD: 0.87; 95%CI: 0.00 to 1.73; P = 0.05), and the serum AMH decreased after 3 months (WMD: -0.26; 95%CI: -0.48 to -0.04; P = 0.02).'

facts.claim: DHEA supplementation (75 mg, 3 times daily) in women with diminished ovarian reserve or poor ovarian response caused a significant increase in serum AMH (WMD 0.18 ng/mL, 95% CI 0.09 to 0.27, P<0.0001).

source.authors: Shrestha R, Nabavi N, Volik S, Anderson S, Haegert A, McConeghy B, Sar F, Brahmbhatt S, Bell R, Le Bihan S, Wang Y, Collins C, Churg A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Using whole exome sequencing of five WDPMs of the peritoneum, we have identified distinct mutations in EHD1, ATM, FBXO10, SH2D2A, CDH5, MAGED1, and TP73 shared by WDPM cases but not reported in malignant mesotheliomas.' Source: Shrestha R et al., Cancers (Basel) 2020;12(6):1568. PMID 32545767; DOI 10.3390/cancers12061568.

facts.claim: WDPM is an uncommon mesothelial proliferation that is most commonly encountered as an incidental finding in the peritoneal cavity; it is genetically distinct from malignant mesothelioma.

source.title: Well-Differentiated Papillary Mesothelioma of the Peritoneum Is Genetically Distinct from Malignant Mesothelioma

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Well-differentiated papillary mesothelioma (WDPM) is an uncommon mesothelial proliferation that is most commonly encountered as an incidental finding in the peritoneal cavity.' And study title: 'Well-Differentiated Papillary Mesothelioma of the Peritoneum Is Genetically Distinct from Malignant Mesothelioma.' Source: Shrestha R et al., Cancers 2020, PMID 32545767.

facts.claim: WDPM is strongly enriched with C>A transversion substitution mutations — a pattern not found in malignant mesothelioma.

facts.claim: Lifestyle intervention reducing body weight by as little as 5% has been shown to have health, metabolic, reproductive, and psychological benefits in women with PCOS and excess weight and is considered the first-line treatment for overweight/obese anovulatory PCOS women.

facts.claim: When presented with equal pregnancy rates and asked to choose between natural fertilization in the woman's body versus laboratory fertilization, 69% of U.S. respondents preferred natural fertilization (RRM) versus 17% for lab fertilization (IVF) in a nationally representative survey (N=1000).

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Descriptive analysis indicated that 69% of respondents selected Option X, while 17% preferred Option Y. A Chi-Square test confirmed a statistically significant difference in the distribution of responses, χ² (2) = 573.16, p < 0.05, indicating a greater preference for Option X compared with Option Y.' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, N=1000. Option X = identify and treat underlying cause for natural fertilization; Option Y = bypass cause, lab fertilization. Pregnancy rates stated as equal.

facts.claim: In a U.S. national survey (N=1002), only 14% of respondents (n=145) claimed to be very familiar with IVF, while 13% (n=133) were not familiar at all and 31% (n=310) had heard of IVF but knew very little about it.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'However, only 14% of respondents (n=145) claimed to be very familiar with IVF, 41% (n=414) were somewhat familiar, 13% were not familiar at all (n=133) and 31% had heard of IVF but knew very little about it (n=310).' Context: J.L. Partners survey, N=1002.

facts.claim: In a U.S. national survey (N=1002), 53% of respondents (n=531) preferred 'treating the root issue which is causing the symptom of infertility' over 'using IVF as a first line treatment' which was preferred by only 9% (n=86); 26% preferred a combination.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Treating the root issue which is causing the symptom of infertility was preferred over using IVF as a first line treatment, 53% (n=531) vs 9% (n=86), with 26% (n=263) choosing a combination of both and 12% (n=159) being unsure.' Context: J.L. Partners survey, N=1002.

facts.claim: 82% of U.S. survey respondents (N=1000) agreed that a comprehensive work-up to identify and understand underlying contributing factors to infertility was very important.

facts.claim: 73% of U.S. survey respondents supported transparency and standardization in how IVF clinics report their data.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'There was an overwhelming support for transparency and standardization in how IVF clinics report their data (73%).' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, N=1000.

facts.claim: Support for IVF declined 10 percentage points (from 81.5% to 71.4%) in a U.S. national survey (N=1002) after respondents were presented with information about medical risks, embryo creation/storage, preimplantation genetic testing, and arguments for government oversight.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'the proportion of respondents expressing support for IVF declined 10 percentage points from 81.5% to 71.4%. A chi-square test of independence confirmed a statistically significant shift in the overall distribution of opinions across the categories of Support, Oppose, and Unsure, χ² (2) = 33.28, p < 0.001. The effect size (Cramér's V = 0.129) indicates a small to moderate shift.' Context: J.L. Partners survey, N=1002, pre/post design treating responses as independent samples.

facts.claim: 43% of U.S. survey respondents (N=1000) had never heard of restorative reproductive medicine, while only 33% viewed it favorably before being given a definition.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Slightly over 40% had not heard of restorative reproductive medicine either, while 33% viewed it favorably.' Figure 6 caption confirms: 'RRM was viewed favorably by 33% (n = 329), while 43% (n = 429) had never heard of it.' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, N=1000, asked before definitions were provided.

facts.claim: After receiving a brief definition of RRM, 79% of U.S. survey respondents (n=788 of 1000) approved of restorative reproductive medicine as a fertility treatment approach.

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys
source.journal: Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: Parnell TA, Copeland K, Minjeur M

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'The McL survey defined RRM as, treatment that focuses on finding and treating the underlying factors causing a couple's fertility problem that aims to restore reproductive function so patients can conceive and have a baby naturally. After this definition, approval for RRM was 79% (n=788).' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, N=1000.

facts.claim: 91% of U.S. survey respondents who had previously undergone IVF approved of RRM after being given a brief definition of the restorative reproductive medicine approach.

facts.claim: 57% of U.S. survey respondents who had undergone IVF reported feeling fast-tracked or pressured into IVF before other treatments were tried, compared with 9% among respondents with infertility/miscarriage history who had not undergone IVF (n=298).

source.title: Public Awareness, Perceptions, and Preferences in Fertility Treatment: Secondary Analysis of Two Public Surveys

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'When asked whether they felt fast-tracked or pressured to consider IVF before other treatments were tried, 57% of those who had undergone IVF reported feeling fast-tracked to IVF. By comparison, among respondents with a history of infertility or miscarriage who had not undergone IVF, 9% (n=298) reported feeling fast-tracked to IVF.' Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey.

facts.claim: 83% of U.S. survey respondents who had undergone IVF (n=76) reported ongoing concern that an undiagnosed underlying health factor contributed to their infertility.

Context: McLaughlin & Associates survey, subgroup of 396 respondents with infertility/miscarriage history; 55% of that full subgroup were concerned about undiagnosed health problems.

facts.claim: When ranking the most important factor in choosing fertility treatment, 74% of U.S. respondents (N=1000) selected the health of the baby as the top priority, versus 13% for cost and 6% for time to conceive.

facts.claim: Nearly half (49%) of U.S. survey respondents reported being unaware of any medical risks associated with IVF, despite documented maternal risks including pre-eclampsia, gestational diabetes, severe maternal morbidity, and caesarean delivery.

facts.claim: Endometriosis prevalence is significantly higher in infertile women (33%) than in previously fertile women undergoing sterilization (4%) based on studies published 1988-2000 (P<0.0001), with moderate-severe disease also more prevalent in the infertile group (32% vs 9%).

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Table 3: Previously fertile N=7953, 300 (4%) with endo; Infertile N=2372, 781 (33%) with endo. P<0.0001 for both prevalence and moderate-severe distribution. Author quote: 'infertile women have a significantly higher prevalence of endometriosis (P<0.0001) and are significantly more likely to have moderate to severe endometriosis (P<0.0001) than previously fertile women.'

facts.claim: The monthly fecundity rate (MFR) in untreated women with minimal to mild endometriosis is approximately 2-4%, compared to an MFR of 15-20% in women with normal fertility, demonstrating significant fertility impairment even with mild disease.

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'the MFR was 8.7% in patients with no other identifiable infertility factors, which is similar to the estimated MFR of 5%, 10%, 11%, and 8% in patients with mild endometriosis evaluated in other studies.' Endocan study control group MFR: 2.4%. Berube 1998 estimate: <2.5% for min-mild endo vs ~3.48% for unexplained infertility.

facts.claim: A meta-analysis of 5214 IUI cycles demonstrated that the odds ratio for pregnancy in women with minimal-mild endometriosis was 0.45 (95% CI 0.27-0.76), as low as the OR for couples with male factor infertility (0.48, 95% CI 0.37-0.61).

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'In a meta-analysis of 5214 cycles of high intrauterine insemination (IUI), using stepwise logistic regression, it was demonstrated that the odds ratio for pregnancy associated with minimal to mild endometriosis was 0.45, significantly reduced (95% CI, 0.27-0.76) compared with women without endometriosis and as low as the odds ratio (0.48) for couples with male factor infertility (95% CI, 0.37-0.61).'

facts.claim: The Canadian ENDOCAN randomized controlled trial (n=341) demonstrated that surgical excision of minimal-mild endometriosis nearly doubled the monthly fecundity rate (4.7% vs 2.4% control; rate ratio 1.9, 95% CI 1.2-3.1) and increased the cumulative pregnancy rate from 17.7% to 30.7% (P=0.006).

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Table 7: Endocan 1997 RCT, N=341, 2yr duration infertility, no postop GnRH analogs. MFR: 2.4% control, 4.7% excision. Rate ratio 1.9 (95% CI 1.2-3.1). CPR: 17.7% control, 30.7% excision, P=0.006.

facts.claim: Controlled studies of donor insemination confirm that women with minimal to mild endometriosis have significantly lower cycle fecundity rates than women with a normal pelvis: 4% (9/218) vs 20% (38/196) in Hammond 1986, and 6% (5/86) vs 14% in Toma 1992.

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?

facts.verification_notes: Table 6 values from Hammond 1986 and Toma 1992 controlled studies. Donor sperm model eliminates male factor variability and still shows fertility impairment in mild endo.

facts.claim: Olive's MFR analysis in 123 patients with expectant management showed stage-dependent fertility: MFR 8.7% in mild, 3.2% in moderate, and 0% in severe endometriosis; overall pregnancy rates 53%, 25%, and 0% respectively.

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'The MFR in patients with mild, moderate, and severe endometriosis without other identifiable infertility factors was 8.7, 3.2, and 0%, respectively. The overall pregnancy rate in patients with mild, moderate, and severe endometriosis was 53, 25, and 0%, respectively.' Olive et al. n=123, 1-25 months follow-up.

facts.claim: In oocyte donation studies, women with moderate-severe endometriosis who receive donor eggs from women without endometriosis have normal endometrial receptivity, while eggs donated FROM endometriosis patients to women with normal pelvis show reduced embryo quality and implantation rates, indicating endometriosis-associated subfertility is related to egg quality rather than endometrial receptivity.

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'Women with moderate to severe endometriosis who receive donor eggs from women without endometriosis have a normal endometrial receptivity after IVF. In contrast, eggs donated by women with moderate to severe endometriosis to women with a normal pelvis seem to have reduced quality, resulting in reduced embryo quality and reduced implantation rate per embryo. These preliminary data suggest that endometriosis-associated subfertility is related to egg quality, not to endometrial receptivity.'

facts.claim: In the baboon model, monthly fecundity rate was significantly lower in animals with mild, moderate, or severe endometriosis than in those with minimal endometriosis or normal pelvis, with increased luteinized unruptured follicle syndrome suggested as a mechanism.

source.title: Endometriosis and subfertility: is the relationship resolved?
source.journal: Seminars in Reproductive Medicine
source.authors: D'Hooghe TM, Debrock S, Hill JA, Meuleman C

facts.verification_notes: Quote: 'we demonstrated a normal MFR in baboons with spontaneous minimal endometriosis (18%) compared with baboons with a normal pelvis (24%). In one of these studies, we also demonstrated that the MFR was significantly lower in baboons with mild, moderate, or severe endometriosis than in baboons with minimal endometriosis or a normal pelvis. An increased incidence and recurrence of luteinized unruptured follicle (LUF) syndrome were observed in baboons with mild endometriosis.'

facts.claim: A comprehensive andrological, gynecological, and fertility-awareness diagnostic workup followed by targeted etiologic treatment produced a spontaneous pregnancy rate of 40.9% across 919 treated infertile couples (Grande 2025, Italian multicenter cohort, 2015-2022).

facts.claim: Among 1,014 infertile couples seeking natural conception, 9.4% (95 couples) had untreatable causes (secretive azoospermia, premature ovarian failure, or genetic abnormalities) for which no natural-conception pathway could be offered.

facts.claim: Semen parameters within WHO-6 5th-percentile normal ranges were observed in only 13% of men with isolated male factor infertility; the remaining 87% had identifiable seminal abnormalities, demonstrating that semen analysis alone is not sufficient to diagnose or exclude male factor infertility.

facts.claim: A clinical condition associated with male factor infertility was detected in 68% of infertile couples (23% isolated, 45% combined with female factors), underscoring that male infertility assessment is essential in the couple-based workup.

facts.claim: Mild endometriosis (rASRM stage I/II) carried a higher miscarriage rate (42.1%, 95% CI 32.6-51.4) than severe endometriosis (rASRM III/IV: 30.8%, 95% CI 22.6-38.7), with adjusted IRR 1.57 for mild disease (P=.046) but only 1.19 for severe disease (P=.446, not significant) vs controls.

facts.claim: Superficial peritoneal endometriosis (SUP) had the highest miscarriage rate (42.0%, 95% CI 32.0-53.9) with adjusted IRR 1.67 (95% CI 1.07-2.61, P=.024), higher than ovarian endometrioma (28.6%, adjusted IRR 1.11 NS) or deep infiltrating endometriosis (33.9%, adjusted IRR 1.19 NS).

facts.claim: Zero minimum requirement for endometriosis or tubal surgery in REI fellowship; 45% want more surgical training
facts.body: ACGME training requirements for Reproductive Endocrinology and Infertility (REI) fellowship include zero minimum cases for endometriosis surgery or tubal surgery. 45% of REI trainees report wanting additional surgical training, but fellowship programs lack faculty to teach it. Many REI programs refer for reproductive surgery to general gynecologists or MIGS-trained surgeons. Conversely, MIGS (Minimally Invasive Gynecologic Surgery) fellowship has no or minimal fertility-focused training, with emphasis on pain management and hysterectomy. General OBGYN residency also has minimal focus on fertility treatments, adhesion prevention, and endometriosis treatment. This creates a training void that NaPro/RRM surgical fellowship specifically addresses.

source.raw_source_id: ACGME training requirements; Dr. Naomi Whittaker IIRRM lecture (RRM vs ART)

facts.claim: Seven published RRM/NPT studies with cumulative N exceeding 4,000 couples
facts.body: Six published studies on RRM/NPT outcomes: Stanford 2008 (N=1072, 25.5% crude LBR, 24mo). Tham 2012 (N=108, 38.0%, 24mo). Boyle 2018 (N=403, 18.4% crude/32.1% adjusted, post-IVF only). Stanford 2021 (N=370, 17.8%, 24mo). Stanford 2022 (N=834, 44.2%, 36mo). Boyle 2025 (N=187, 41.2%, 32mo). Plus Sanchez-Mendez 2025 (N=1,310, 35.3% crude/62.1% adjusted).

source.raw_source_id: Boyle et al., JRRM, 2025 (Table 8); Sanchez-Mendez et al., 2025

facts.verification_notes: Promoted from local rrm-cli facts.json (170 curated facts)

facts.claim: RRM treatment is reusable for many cycles and future pregnancies
facts.body: RRM treatment addresses underlying health conditions, so improvements persist across future pregnancies without repeating the treatment. IVF is per-cycle -- each attempt is a new treatment episode with full cost and risk exposure. RRM providers often practice obstetrics and follow patients through pregnancy and delivery. REI doctors typically discharge patients back to a regular OBGYN around the second trimester. RRM can also be used preconceptionally in young women to prevent infertility, while conventional medicine requires waiting until infertility is diagnosed before treatment begins. RRM only needs one egg per cycle (optimized), vs. IVF wanting 10-20 eggs (many will be small/poor quality, especially in older ovaries).

source.raw_source_id: Whittaker NM, RRM vs ART lecture

facts.claim: Dose-response: OS 2.17, IUI 3.17, IVF 4.24 adjusted ORs for PTB increase with treatment invasiveness
facts.body: Clear dose-response: as treatment invasiveness increased, both multiple gestation and PTB incidence increased monotonically. Unadjusted ORs for PTB: OS 2.17 (1.08, 4.36), IUI 2.75 (1.35, 5.61), IVF 3.09 (1.74, 5.48). After full adjustment (including diagnosis and duration): OS 2.17 (0.99, 4.75), IUI 3.17 (1.40, 7.19), IVF 4.24 (2.05, 8.77). The adjusted ORs INCREASED for IUI/IVF after controlling for diagnosis, suggesting treatment effect beyond diagnosis. Authors explicitly recommend: 'trying less invasive treatments to achieve pregnancy might reduce their risk of preterm birth.'

source.raw_source_id: Sanders JN et al. Reproductive Health 2022;19:83 (PMID 35351163)

facts.claim: Female factor infertility aOR 2.99 (95% CI 1.50, 5.97) for PTB; male factor not associated; attempt duration not associated
facts.body: A reported diagnosis of female factor infertility independently increased adjusted OR for preterm birth to 2.99 (95% CI 1.50, 5.97). Male factor showed no association: aOR 1.01 (95% CI 0.58, 1.76). This finding persisted in sensitivity analysis excluding tubal factor: aOR 2.75 (95% CI 1.42, 5.31). Duration of pregnancy attempt was NOT independently associated with PTB. This suggests the etiology of subfertility matters more than its severity (measured by duration).

facts.body: In the Fertility Experiences Study (FES, N=490 subfertile women, Utah), adjusted OR for preterm birth <37 weeks: IVF 4.24 (95% CI 2.05, 8.77), IUI 3.17 (95% CI 1.40, 7.19), OS 2.17 (95% CI 0.99, 4.75), all vs subfertile women who conceived without treatment. Adjusted for maternal/paternal age, education, income, religious affiliation, female/male fertility diagnosis, and duration of subfertility. Crucially, the referent group is SUBFERTILE women -- this is not comparing to the general population, making treatment the isolated variable.

facts.claim: Multiple gestation aOR 27.91 (95% CI 13.25, 58.79) per Table 4; text states 28.0 (15.60, 68.60). After adjustment, treatment effects nonsignificant
facts.body: In FES cohort: no treatment 6.6% twins; OS 19% twins + 6% triplets (25% multiples total); IUI 10.9% twins + 5% triplets (15.9% multiples); IVF 30% twins + 2% triplets (32% multiples). When multiple gestation was added to the regression model, it had the strongest association with PTB. WITHIN-PAPER DISCREPANCY: the text (p.6) states aOR 28.0 (95% CI 15.60, 68.60), but Table 4 shows 27.91 (95% CI 13.25, 58.79). The point estimate rounds (27.91 to 28.0) but the confidence intervals differ -- use Table 4 values (27.91, 13.25-58.79) as authoritative. After adjusting for multiples, treatment ORs became nonsignificant: IVF 1.46 (0.59, 3.58), IUI 2.16 (0.82, 5.69), OS 1.34 (0.52, 3.45).

facts.claim: Risk of PTB after OS or IUI is increased on an even greater magnitude to smoking
facts.body: The authors note: 'The risk of PTB after conception using OS or IUI is increased on an even greater magnitude to smoking, yet clinicians and patients may pay less attention to the risk of treatment.' This comparison contextualizes treatment risk: smoking is universally counseled against in pregnancy, yet OS and IUI carry higher relative PTB risk than smoking and receive far less clinical attention as risk factors. This framing supports informed consent for all fertility treatments.

source.raw_source_id: Sanders JN et al. Reproductive Health 2022;19:83 (PMID 35351163)

facts.claim: Singleton analyses not significant for any treatment; overall subfertile cohort PTB rate 19% vs population ~11%
facts.body: When restricted to singleton gestations (84.1% of all births), odds ratios for PTB were not significant for any treatment type. The study lacked statistical power for singleton subanalysis (only 38 singleton preterm births out of 412 singletons, 9.2% rate). Overall PTB rate across the subfertile cohort (19%) was markedly higher than US population rates (~11%). Even subfertile controls had 10.8% PTB, consistent with subfertility itself being a risk factor.
